Pluck strand: primary bead selection from the assigned workspace.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Pluck handles >90% of all bead processing. It queries the bead store for\n4\t//! unassigned, ready beads, filters by excluded labels, and sorts them in\n5\t//! deterministic priority order: `(priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)`.\n6\t//!\n7\t//! Given the same queue state, every worker computes the same candidate list.\n8\t\n9\tuse crate::bead_store::{BeadStore, Filters};\n10\tuse crate::types::{Bead, StrandError, StrandResult};\n11\t\n12\t/// Default labels excluded from Pluck selection when not configured.\n13\tconst DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_LABELS: &[&str] = &[\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"];\n14\t\n15\t/// The Pluck strand — primary work selection.\n16\tpub struct PluckStrand {\n17\t    /// Labels to exclude from candidate selection.\n18\t    exclude_labels: Vec<String>,\n19\t    /// Auto-split beads after this many consecutive failures (0 = disabled).\n20\t    split_after_failures: u32,\n21\t}\n22\t\n23\timpl PluckStrand {\n24\t    /// Create a new PluckStrand with the given exclude labels.\n25\t    ///\n26\t    /// If `exclude_labels` is empty, the default set (`deferred`, `human`,\n27\t    /// `blocked`) is used.\n28\t    pub fn new(exclude_labels: Vec<String>) -> Self {\n29\t        let labels else {\n35\t            exclude_labels\n36\t        };\n37\t        PluckStrand {\n38\t            exclude_labels: labels,\n39\t            split_after_failures: 3, // default threshold\n40\t        }\n41\t    }\n42\t\n43\t    /// Create a new PluckStrand with the given exclude labels and split threshold.\n44\t    ///\n45\t    /// If `exclude_labels` is empty, the default set (`deferred`, `human`,\n46\t    /// `blocked`) is used.\n47\t    pub fn with_split_threshold(exclude_labels: Vec<String>, split_after_failures: u32) -> Self {\n48\t        let labels = if exclude_labels.is_empty() {\n49\t            DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_LABELS\n50\t                .iter()\n51\t                .map(|s| (*s).to_string())\n52\t                .collect()\n53\t        } else {\n54\t            exclude_labels\n55\t        };\n56\t        PluckStrand {\n57\t            exclude_labels: labels,\n58\t            split_after_failures,\n59\t        }\n60\t    }\n61\t\n62\t    /// Extract the failure count from a bead's labels.\n63\t    ///\n64\t    /// Labels follow the pattern `failure-count:N`. Returns the count if found,\n65\t    /// or 0 if no failure-count label exists.\n66\t    fn extract_failure_count(bead: &Bead) -> u32 {\n67\t        bead.labels\n68\t            .iter()\n69\t            .filter_map(|l| l.strip_prefix(\"failure-count:\"))\n70\t            .filter_map(|s| s.parse::<u32>().ok())\n71\t            .max()\n72\t            .unwrap_or(0)\n73\t    }\n74\t\n75\t    /// Sort candidates in deterministic priority order.\n76\t    ///\n77\t    /// Sort key: `(priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)`.\n78\t    /// The id tie-breaker ensures identical ordering across platforms.\n79\t    fn sort_candidates(candidates: &mut [Bead]) {\n80\t        candidates.sort_by(|a, b| {\n81\t            a.priority\n82\t                .cmp(&b.priority)\n83\t                .then_with(|| a.created_at.cmp(&b.created_at))\n84\t                .then_with(|| a.id.as_ref().cmp(b.id.as_ref()))\n85\t        });\n86\t    }\n87\t}\n88\t\n89\t#[async_trait::async_trait]\n90\timpl super::Strand for PluckStrand {\n91\t    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n92\t        \"pluck\"\n93\t    }\n94\t\n95\t    #[tracing::instrument(\n96\t        name = \"strand.pluck\",\n97\t        skip(self, store),\n98\t        fields(\n99\t            strand = \"pluck\",\n100\t            exclude_labels = ?self.exclude_labels,\n101\t            split_threshold = self.split_after_failures,\n102\t        )\n103\t    )]\n104\t    async fn evaluate(&self, store: &dyn BeadStore) -> StrandResult {\n105\t        tracing::debug!(\n106\t            exclude_labels = ?self.exclude_labels,\n107\t            split_threshold = self.split_after_failures,\n108\t            \"Pluck strand evaluation starting\"\n109\t        );\n110\t\n111\t        // 1. Query bead store for ready, unassigned beads.\n112\t        let filters = Filters {\n113\t            assignee: None,\n114\t            exclude_labels: self.exclude_labels.clone(),\n115\t        };\n116\t\n117\t        tracing::debug!(\n118\t            filters = ?filters,\n119\t            \"Querying bead store for ready candidates\"\n120\t        );\n121\t\n122\t        let mut candidates = match store.ready(&filters).await {\n123\t            Ok(beads) => {\n124\t                tracing::debug!(\n125\t                    count = beads.len(),\n126\t                    \"Bead store returned {} candidates\",\n127\t                    beads.len()\n128\t                );\n129\t                beads\n130\t            }\n131\t            Err(e) => {\n132\t                tracing::error!(\n133\t                    error = %e,\n134\t                    \"Bead store query failed\"\n135\t                );\n136\t                // Bead store error is semantically different from NoWork.\n137\t                return StrandResult::Error(StrandError::StoreError(e));\n138\t            }\n139\t        };\n140\t\n141\t        // 2. Filter: remove beads with excluded labels.\n142\t        //    Defensive guard — store.ready() passes exclude_labels in its Filters,\n143\t        //    but the backing CLI may not include label data in every query type.\n144\t        //    Filtering here guarantees excluded-label beads are never presented as\n145\t        //    candidates regardless of backend behaviour, preventing the\n146\t        //    SELECTING→CLAIMING→RETRYING spin loop observed when br ready --json\n147\t        //    omits label fields for some beads.\n148\t        let before_label_filter = candidates.len();\n149\t        candidates.retain(|b| !b.labels.iter().any(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l)));\n150\t        let after_label_filter = candidates.len();\n151\t\n152\t        if before_label_filter != after_label_filter {\n153\t            tracing::debug!(\n154\t                excluded_count = before_label_filter - after_label_filter,\n155\t                remaining = after_label_filter,\n156\t                excluded_labels = ?self.exclude_labels,\n157\t                \"Label filtering excluded {} beads\",\n158\t                before_label_filter - after_label_filter\n159\t            );\n160\t\n161\t            // Log each excluded bead at DEBUG level\n162\t            let excluded_beads: Vec<_> = candidates.iter()\n163\t                .filter(|b| b.labels.iter().any(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l)))\n164\t                .map(|b| (b.id.as_ref(), b.labels.clone()))\n165\t                .collect();\n166\t            for (id, labels) in excluded_beads {\n167\t                let excluded_reasons: Vec<_> = labels.iter()\n168\t                    .filter(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l))\n169\t                    .map(|l| l.as_str())\n170\t                    .collect();\n171\t                tracing::debug!(\n172\t                    bead_id = %id,\n173\t                    labels = ?labels,\n174\t                    excluded_reasons = ?excluded_reasons,\n175\t                    \"Excluded bead due to labels\"\n176\t                );\n177\t            }\n178\t\n179\t            // Re-filter since we logged them\n180\t            candidates.retain(|b| !b.labels.iter().any(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l)));\n181\t        } else {\n182\t            tracing::debug!(\n183\t                count = after_label_filter,\n184\t                \"No beads excluded by label filter\"\n185\t            );\n186\t        }\n187\t\n188\t        // 3. Filter: remove beads that are actively in_progress (claimed by another worker)\n189\t        //    and Open beads with a stale assignee. These are never claimable — the claimer\n190\t        //    will reject them every time, causing a hot loop.\n191\t        let before_status_filter = candidates.len();\n192\t        candidates.retain(|b| {\n193\t            !(matches!(b.status, crate::types::BeadStatus::InProgress)\n194\t                || (b.status == crate::types::BeadStatus::Open && b.assignee.is_some()))\n195\t        });\n196\t        let after_status_filter = candidates.len();\n197\t\n198\t        if before_status_filter != after_status_filter {\n199\t            tracing::debug!(\n200\t                filtered_count = before_status_filter - after_status_filter,\n201\t                remaining = after_status_filter,\n202\t                \"Status/assignee filtering removed {} beads\",\n203\t                before_status_filter - after_status_filter\n204\t            );\n205\t        } else {\n206\t            tracing::debug!(\n207\t                count = after_status_filter,\n208\t                \"No beads excluded by status/assignee filter\"\n209\t            );\n210\t        }\n211\t\n212\t        // 4. Sort: deterministic (priority, created_at, id).\n213\t        if !candidates.is_empty() {\n214\t            let first = &candidates[0];\n215\t            tracing::debug!(\n216\t                total = candidates.len(),\n217\t                first_bead_id = %first.id,\n218\t                first_priority = first.priority,\n219\t                first_created_at = %first.created_at,\n220\t                \"Sorting {} candidates by (priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)\",\n221\t                candidates.len()\n222\t            );\n223\t        }\n224\t        Self::sort_candidates(&mut candidates);\n225\t\n226\t        // 5. Check for split trigger: if the first candidate has accumulated\n227\t        //    enough consecutive failures, dispatch a SPLIT instruction instead\n228\t        //    of returning the bead for normal processing.\n229\t        if self.split_after_failures > 0 {\n230\t            if let Some(first_candidate) = candidates.first() {\n231\t                let failure_count = Self::extract_failure_count(first_candidate);\n232\t                tracing::debug!(\n233\t                    bead_id = %first_candidate.id,\n234\t                    failure_count = failure_count,\n235\t                    threshold = self.split_after_failures,\n236\t                    split_triggered = failure_count >= self.split_after_failures,\n237\t                    \"Checking split trigger for first candidate\"\n238\t                );\n239\t\n240\t                if failure_count >= self.split_after_failures {\n241\t                    tracing::info!(\n242\t                        bead_id = %first_candidate.id,\n243\t                        failure_count = failure_count,\n244\t                        threshold = self.split_after_failures,\n245\t                        \"Split threshold reached, returning Split instruction\"\n246\t                    );\n247\t                    return StrandResult::Split(Box::new(first_candidate.clone()), failure_count);\n248\t                }\n249\t            }\n250\t        } else {\n251\t            tracing::debug!(\"Split trigger disabled (threshold = 0)\");\n252\t        }\n253\t\n254\t        // 6. Return result.\n255\t        if candidates.is_empty() {\n256\t            tracing::debug!(\"No candidates remaining after filtering, returning NoWork\");\n257\t            StrandResult::NoWork\n258\t        } else {\n259\t            let candidate_ids: Vec<&str> = candidates.iter()\n260\t                .map(|b| b.id.as_ref())\n261\t                .collect();\n262\t            tracing::info!(\n263\t                count = candidates.len(),\n264\t                candidates = ?candidate_ids,\n265\t                \"Returning {} candidates for processing\",\n266\t                candidates.len()\n267\t            );\n268\t            StrandResult::BeadFound(candidates)\n269\t        }\n270\t    }\n271\t}\n272\t\n273\t// ─── Unit tests }\n542\t\n543\t    fn make_bead(id: &str, priority: u8, created_at: &str) -> Bead {\n544\t        let dt = chrono::NaiveDateTime::parse_from_str(created_at, \"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\")\n545\t dependencies: vec![],\n556\t            dependents: vec![],\n557\t            created_at: Utc.from_utc_datetime(&dt),\n558\t            updated_at: Utc.from_utc_datetime(&dt),\n559\t        }\n560\t    }\n561\t\n562\t    fn make_bead_with_labels(id: &str, priority: u8, labels: Vec<&str>) -> Bead {\n563\t        let mut bead = make_bead(id, priority, \"2026-01-01 00:00:00\");\n564\t // ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n577\t    // Sorting\n578\t    // ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n579\t\n580\t    #[tokio::test]\n581\t    async fn candidates_sorted_by_priority_then_created_at() {\n582\t        let store = MemoryStore {\n583\t            beads: vec![\n584\t #[tokio::test]\n603\t    async fn tie_broken_by_bead_id() {\n604\t        // Same priority, same created_at — tie broken by id (lexicographic).\n605\t        let store = MemoryStore {\n606\t            beads: vec![\n607\t                make_bead(\"bbb\", 1, \"2026-01-01 00:00:00\"),\n608\t                make_bead(\"aaa\", 1, \"2026-01-01 00:00:00\"),\n609\t                make_bead(\"ccc\", 1, \"2026-01-01 00:00:00\"),\n610\t            ],\n611\t        };\n612\t\n613\t        let strand = PluckStrand::new(vec![]);\n614\t        let result = strand.evaluate(&store).await;\n615\t\n616\t ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n626\t    // Filtering\n627\t    // ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n628\t\n629\t    #[tokio::test]\n630\t    async fn beads_with_excluded_labels_are_filtered() {\n631\t        let store = MemoryStore {\n632\t            beads: vec![\n633\t                make_bead_with_labels(\"deferred-bead\", 1, vec![\"deferred\"]),\n634\t                make_bead_with_labels(\"human-bead\", 1, vec![\"human\"]),\n635\t                make_bead_with_labels(\"blocked-bead\", 1, vec![\"blocked\"]),\n636\t                make_bead_with_labels(\"normal-bead\", 1, vec![]),\n637\t            ],\n638\t        };\n639\t\n640\t        let }\n916\t}\n917\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"f529f17d-ba9c-4a14-bc7d-2aec286f9f85","uuid":"6dddba23-51a3-4f16-b15c-efb7421ce5fa","timestamp":"2026-07-09T04:42:49.329Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/strand/pluck.rs","content":"//! Pluck strand: primary bead selection from the assigned workspace.\n//!\n//! Pluck handles >90% of all bead processing. It queries the bead store for\n//! unassigned, ready beads, filters by excluded labels, and sorts them in\n//! deterministic priority order: `(priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)`.\n//!\n//! Given the same queue state, every worker computes the same candidate list.\n\nuse crate::bead_store::{BeadStore, Filters};\nuse crate::types::{Bead, StrandError, StrandResult};\n\n/// Default labels excluded from Pluck selection when not configured.\nconst DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_LABELS: &[&str] = &[\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"];\n\n/// The Pluck strand — primary work selection.\npub struct PluckStrand {\n    /// Labels to exclude from candidate selection.\n    exclude_labels: Vec<String>,\n    /// Auto-split beads after this many consecutive failures (0 = disabled).\n    split_after_failures: u32,\n}\n\nimpl PluckStrand {\n    /// Create a new PluckStrand with the given exclude labels.\n    ///\n    /// If `exclude_labels` is empty, the default set (`deferred`, `human`,\n    /// `blocked`) is used.\n    pub fn new(exclude_labels: Vec<String>) -> Self {\n        let labels = if exclude_labels.is_empty() {\n            DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_LABELS\n                .iter()\n                .map(|s| (*s).to_string())\n                .collect()\n        } else {\n            exclude_labels\n        };\n        PluckStrand {\n            exclude_labels: labels,\n            split_after_failures: 3, // default threshold\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new PluckStrand with the given exclude labels and split threshold.\n    ///\n    /// If `exclude_labels` is empty, the default set (`deferred`, `human`,\n    /// `blocked`) is used.\n    pub fn with_split_threshold(exclude_labels: Vec<String>, split_after_failures: u32) -> Self {\n        let labels = if exclude_labels.is_empty() {\n            DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_LABELS\n                .iter()\n                .map(|s| (*s).to_string())\n                .collect()\n        } else {\n            exclude_labels\n        };\n        PluckStrand {\n            exclude_labels: labels,\n            split_after_failures,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Extract the failure count from a bead's labels.\n    ///\n    /// Labels follow the pattern `failure-count:N`. Returns the count if found,\n    /// or 0 if no failure-count label exists.\n    fn extract_failure_count(bead: &Bead) -> u32 {\n        bead.labels\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|l| l.strip_prefix(\"failure-count:\"))\n            .filter_map(|s| s.parse::<u32>().ok())\n            .max()\n            .unwrap_or(0)\n    }\n\n    /// Sort candidates in deterministic priority order.\n    ///\n    /// Sort key: `(priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)`.\n    /// The id tie-breaker ensures identical ordering across platforms.\n    fn sort_candidates(candidates: &mut [Bead]) {\n        candidates.sort_by(|a, b| {\n            a.priority\n                .cmp(&b.priority)\n                .then_with(|| a.created_at.cmp(&b.created_at))\n                .then_with(|| a.id.as_ref().cmp(b.id.as_ref()))\n        });\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait::async_trait]\nimpl super::Strand for PluckStrand {\n    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n        \"pluck\"\n    }\n\n    #[tracing::instrument(\n        name = \"strand.pluck\",\n        skip(self, store),\n        fields(\n            strand = \"pluck\",\n            exclude_labels = ?self.exclude_labels,\n            split_threshold = self.split_after_failures,\n        )\n    )]\n    async fn evaluate(&self, store: &dyn BeadStore) -> StrandResult {\n        tracing::debug!(\n            exclude_labels = ?self.exclude_labels,\n            split_threshold = self.split_after_failures,\n            \"Pluck strand evaluation starting\"\n        );\n\n        // 1. Query bead store for ready, unassigned beads.\n        let filters = Filters {\n            assignee: None,\n            exclude_labels: self.exclude_labels.clone(),\n        };\n\n        tracing::debug!(\n            filters = ?filters,\n            \"Querying bead store for ready candidates\"\n        );\n\n        let mut candidates = match store.ready(&filters).await {\n            Ok(beads) => {\n                tracing::debug!(\n                    count = beads.len(),\n                    \"Bead store returned {} candidates\",\n                    beads.len()\n                );\n                beads\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::error!(\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"Bead store query failed\"\n                );\n                // Bead store error is semantically different from NoWork.\n                return StrandResult::Error(StrandError::StoreError(e));\n            }\n        };\n\n        // 2. Filter: remove beads with excluded labels.\n        //    Defensive guard — store.ready() passes exclude_labels in its Filters,\n        //    but the backing CLI may not include label data in every query type.\n        //    Filtering here guarantees excluded-label beads are never presented as\n        //    candidates regardless of backend behaviour, preventing the\n        //    SELECTING→CLAIMING→RETRYING spin loop observed when br ready --json\n        //    omits label fields for some beads.\n        let before_label_filter = candidates.len();\n        candidates.retain(|b| !b.labels.iter().any(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l)));\n        let after_label_filter = candidates.len();\n\n        if before_label_filter != after_label_filter {\n            tracing::debug!(\n                excluded_count = before_label_filter - after_label_filter,\n                remaining = after_label_filter,\n                excluded_labels = ?self.exclude_labels,\n                \"Label filtering excluded {} beads\",\n                before_label_filter - after_label_filter\n            );\n\n            // Log each excluded bead at DEBUG level\n            let excluded_beads: Vec<_> = candidates.iter()\n                .filter(|b| b.labels.iter().any(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l)))\n                .map(|b| (b.id.as_ref(), b.labels.clone()))\n                .collect();\n            for (id, labels) in excluded_beads {\n                let excluded_reasons: Vec<_> = labels.iter()\n                    .filter(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l))\n                    .map(|l| l.as_str())\n                    .collect();\n                tracing::debug!(\n                    bead_id = %id,\n                    labels = ?labels,\n                    excluded_reasons = ?excluded_reasons,\n                    \"Excluded bead due to labels\"\n                );\n            }\n\n            // Re-filter since we logged them\n            candidates.retain(|b| !b.labels.iter().any(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l)));\n        } else {\n            tracing::debug!(\n                count = after_label_filter,\n                \"No beads excluded by label filter\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        // 3. Filter: remove beads that are actively in_progress (claimed by another worker)\n        //    and Open beads with a stale assignee. These are never claimable — the claimer\n        //    will reject them every time, causing a hot loop.\n        let before_status_filter = candidates.len();\n        candidates.retain(|b| {\n            !(matches!(b.status, crate::types::BeadStatus::InProgress)\n                || (b.status == crate::types::BeadStatus::Open && b.assignee.is_some()))\n        });\n        let after_status_filter = candidates.len();\n\n        if before_status_filter != after_status_filter {\n            tracing::debug!(\n                filtered_count = before_status_filter - after_status_filter,\n                remaining = after_status_filter,\n                \"Status/assignee filtering removed {} beads\",\n                before_status_filter - after_status_filter\n            );\n        } else {\n            tracing::debug!(\n                count = after_status_filter,\n                \"No beads excluded by status/assignee filter\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        // 4. Sort: deterministic (priority, created_at, id).\n        if !candidates.is_empty() {\n            let first = &candidates[0];\n            tracing::debug!(\n                total = candidates.len(),\n                first_bead_id = %first.id,\n                first_priority = first.priority,\n                first_created_at = %first.created_at,\n                \"Sorting {} candidates by (priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)\",\n                candidates.len()\n            );\n        }\n        Self::sort_candidates(&mut candidates);\n\n        // 5. Check for split trigger: if the first candidate has accumulated\n        //    enough consecutive failures, dispatch a SPLIT instruction instead\n        //    of returning the bead for normal processing.\n        if self.split_after_failures > 0 {\n            if let Some(first_candidate) = candidates.first() {\n                let failure_count = Self::extract_failure_count(first_candidate);\n                tracing::debug!(\n                    bead_id = %first_candidate.id,\n                    failure_count = failure_count,\n                    threshold = self.split_after_failures,\n                    split_triggered = failure_count >= self.split_after_failures,\n                    \"Checking split trigger for first candidate\"\n                );\n\n                if failure_count >= self.split_after_failures {\n                    tracing::info!(\n                        bead_id = %first_candidate.id,\n                        failure_count = failure_count,\n                        threshold = self.split_after_failures,\n                        \"Split threshold reached, returning Split instruction\"\n                    );\n                    return StrandResult::Split(Box::new(first_candidate.clone()), failure_count);\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            tracing::debug!(\"Split trigger disabled (threshold = 0)\");\n        }\n\n        // 6. Return result.\n        if candidates.is_empty() {\n            tracing::debug!(\"No candidates remaining after filtering, returning NoWork\");\n            StrandResult::NoWork\n        } else {\n            let candidate_ids: Vec<&str> = candidates.iter()\n                .map(|b| b.id.as_ref())\n                .collect();\n            tracing::info!(\n                count = candidates.len(),\n                candidates = ?candidate_ids,\n                \"Returning {} candidates for processing\",\n                candidates.len()\n            );\n            StrandResult::BeadFound(candidates)\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ─── Unit tests ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::bead_store::RepairReport;\n    use crate::types::{BeadId, BeadStatus, ClaimResult};\n\n    use anyhow::Result;\n    use chrono::{TimeZone, Utc};\n    use std::path::PathBuf;\n\n    /// In-memory bead store for testing.\n    struct MemoryStore {\n        beads: Vec<Bead>,\n    }\n\n    #[async_trait::async_trait]\n    impl BeadStore for MemoryStore {\n        async fn list_all(&self) -> Result<Vec<Bead>> {\n            Ok(self.beads.clone())\n        }\n        async fn ready(&self, filters: &Filters) -> Result<Vec<Bead>> {\n            let result: Vec<Bead> = self\n                .beads\n                .iter()\n                .filter(|b| {\n                    // Filter by assignee if set.\n                    if let Some(ref a) = filters.assignee {\n                        if b.assignee.as_ref() != Some(a) {\n                            return false;\n                        }\n                    }\n                    // Filter out beads with excluded labels.\n                    if b.labels.iter().any(|l| filters.exclude_labels.contains(l)) {\n                        return false;\n                    }\n                    true\n                })\n                .cloned()\n                .collect();\n            Ok(result)\n        }\n\n        async fn show(&self, id: &BeadId) -> Result<Bead> {\n            self.beads\n                .iter()\n                .find(|b| b.id == *id)\n                .cloned()\n                .ok_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!(\"bead not found: {id}\"))\n        }\n\n        async fn claim(&self, _id: &BeadId, _actor: &str) -> Result<ClaimResult> {\n            anyhow::bail!(\"not implemented\")\n        }\n\n        async fn release(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n\n        async fn flush(&self) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n\n        async fn reopen(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n\n        async fn labels(&self, id: &BeadId) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\n            let bead = self.show(id).await?;\n            Ok(bead.labels)\n        }\n\n        async fn add_label(&self, _id: &BeadId, _label: &str) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n\n        async fn remove_label(&self, _id: &BeadId, _label: &str) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n\n        async fn create_bead(&self, _title: &str, _body: &str, _labels: &[&str]) -> Result<BeadId> {\n            Ok(BeadId::from(\"new-bead\".to_string()))\n        }\n\n        async fn doctor_repair(&self) -> Result<RepairReport> {\n            Ok(RepairReport::default())\n        }\n        async fn doctor_check(&self) -> Result<RepairReport> {\n            Ok(RepairReport::default())\n        }\n        async fn full_rebuild(&self) -> NoWork causes the worker\n        // to move to Exhausted rather than spinning the claim-retry loop.\n        let store = UnfilteredStore {\n            beads: vec![\n ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n46\t\n47\t/// Global pointer to the shutdown flag, used by synchronous signal handlers.\n48\t/// This is necessary because signal handlers run in a separate context and\n49\t/// cannot easily access the Worker's shutdown flag directly.\n50\t#[cfg(unix)]\n51\tstatic GLOBAL_SHUTDOWN_FLAG: AtomicUsize = AtomicUsize::new(0);\n52\t\n53\t/// Set the global shutdown flag pointer.\n54\t/// Called by `install_signal_handlers` to register the shutdown flag.\n55\t#[cfg(unix)]\n56\tfn set_global_shutdown_flag(ptr: usize) {\n57\t    GLOBAL_SHUTDOWN_FLAG.store(ptr, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n58\t}\n59\t\n60\t/// Clear the global shutdown flag pointer.\n61\t/// Called when the worker is dropped to avoid dangling pointers.\n62\t#[cfg(unix)]\n63\tfn clear_global_shutdown_flag() {\n64\t    GLOBAL_SHUTDOWN_FLAG.store(0, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n65\t    LAST_SIGNAL.store(0, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n66\t}\n67\t\n68\t/// Track the last received signal for diagnostic logging.\n69\t/// AtomicU32 allows lock-free reads/writes from the signal handler.\n70\t#[cfg(unix)]\n71\tstatic LAST_SIGNAL: AtomicU32 = AtomicU32::new(0);\n72\t\n73\t/// Synchronous signal handler for SIGTERM, SIGINT, and SIGHUP.\n74\t///\n75\t/// This function is called directly by the OS when a signal is received.\n76\t/// It must be async-signal-safe: no allocation, no locking, no I/O.\n77\t/// We set the atomic shutdown flag, record the signal number, and return immediately.\n78\t#[cfg(unix)]\n79\textern \"C\" fn signal_handler(sig: i32) {\n80\t    // SAFETY: The signal handler is only installed after set_global_shutdown_flag\n81\t    // has been called with a valid pointer. The pointer remains valid for the\n82\t    // entire lifetime of the worker process.\n83\t    let ptr = GLOBAL_SHUTDOWN_FLAG.load(AtomicOrdering::SeqCst) as *const AtomicBool;\n84\t    if !ptr.is_null() {\n85\t        // SAFETY: The pointer is valid and points to an AtomicBool that lives\n86\t        // for the entire program duration.\n87\t        unsafe {\n88\t            (*ptr).store(true, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n89\t        }\n90\t        // Record the signal number so the main loop can log it.\n91\t        LAST_SIGNAL.store(sig as u32, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n92\t    }\n93\t}\n94\t\n95\t/// Install synchronous signal handlers for SIGTERM, SIGINT, and SIGHUP.\n96\t///\n97\t/// Uses libc::sigaction to register handlers that set the shutdown flag\n98\t/// immediately when a signal is received. This ensures that signals are\n99\t/// caught even if the tokio runtime hasn't polled async signal tasks yet.\n100\t#[cfg(unix)]\n101\tunsafe fn install_unix_signal_handlers() {\n102\t    use libc::{sigaction, sigemptyset, SA_RESTART, SIGHUP, SIGINT, SIGTERM};\n103\t\n104\t    // Set up the sigaction structure.\n105\t    let mut act: libc::sigaction = std::mem::zeroed();\n106\t    act.sa_sigaction = signal_handler as *const () as usize;\n107\t    // Block all signals during handler execution to prevent re-entrancy issues.\n108\t    sigemptyset(&mut act.sa_mask as *mut libc::sigset_t);\n109\t    // Use SA_RESTART to automatically restart system calls interrupted by signals.\n110\t    act.sa_flags = SA_RESTART;\n111\t\n112\t    // Install handlers for SIGTERM, SIGINT, and SIGHUP.\n113\t    // We ignore errors here - if a handler can't be installed, we'll log a\n114\t    // warning but continue. The async handlers (below) provide a fallback.\n115\t    for &sig in &[SIGTERM, SIGINT, SIGHUP] {\n116\t        let mut old: libc::sigaction = std::mem::zeroed();\n117\t        if sigaction(sig, &act, &mut old) == 0 {\n118\t            tracing::debug!(signal = sig, \"installed synchronous signal handler\");\n119\t        } else {\n120\t            // Log the error but don't fail - the async handlers provide a fallback.\n121\t            tracing::warn!(\n122\t                signal = sig,\n123\t                errno = *libc::__errno_location(),\n124\t                \"failed to install synchronous signal handler\"\n125\t            );\n126\t        }\n127\t    }\n128\t}\n129\t\n130\t/// Global state for atexit handler to emit worker.stopped on unexpected termination.\n131\t///\n132\t/// When a worker is killed externally (e.g., SIGKILL from capacity governor),\n133\t/// the normal signal handlers don't run. The atexit handler provides a last-resort\n134\t/// mechanism to emit worker.stopped telemetry with diagnostic information.\n135\tstatic ATEXIT_WORKER_STATE: Mutex<Option<AtexitWorkerState>> = Mutex::new(None);\n136\t\n137\t/// State captured for the atexit handler.\n138\tstruct AtexitWorkerState {\n139\t    worker_name: String,\n140\t    beads_processed: u64,\n141\t    start_time: Instant,\n142\t    last_state: String,\n143\t    log_file_path: Option<String>,\n144\t    /// Path to this worker's heartbeat file for cleanup on unexpected termination.\n145\t    heartbeat_path: Option<String>,\n146\t}\n147\t\n148\t/// Register the atexit handler with worker state.\n149\t///\n150\t/// Called by `install_signal_handlers` to ensure the atexit handler can\n151\t/// emit meaningful telemetry if the process terminates unexpectedly.\n152\tfn register_atexit_handler(\n153\t    worker_name: String,\n154\t    beads_processed: u64,\n155\t    start_time: Instant,\n156\t    last_state: String,\n157\t    log_file_path: Option<String>,\n158\t    heartbeat_path: Option<String>,\n159\t) {\n160\t    let state = AtexitWorkerState {\n161\t        worker_name,\n162\t        beads_processed,\n163\t        start_time,\n164\t        last_state,\n165\t        log_file_path,\n166\t        heartbeat_path,\n167\t    };\n168\t    *ATEXIT_WORKER_STATE.lock().unwrap() = Some(state);\n169\t\n170\t    // Register the atexit handler.\n171\t    // This will run when the process exits normally, but NOT on SIGKILL.\n172\t    extern \"C\" fn atexit_handler() {\n173\t        if let Some(state) = ATEXIT_WORKER_STATE.lock().unwrap().as_ref() {\n174\t            let uptime = state.start_time.elapsed().as_secs();\n175\t            // Try to write to stderr as a last resort since telemetry may be unavailable.\n176\t            eprintln!(\n177\t                \"NEEDLE worker '{}' stopped unexpectedly: state={}, beads_processed={}, uptime={}s\",\n178\t                state.worker_name, state.last_state, state.beads_processed, uptime\n179\t            );\n180\t            eprintln!(\"This indicates the worker was killed by an external process (e.g., SIGKILL, OOM, capacity governor)\");\n181\t\n182\t            // Try to clean up the heartbeat file to prevent stale detection.\n183\t            // Use the proper cleanup function with error handling from the health module.\n184\t            if let Some(ref hb_path) = state.heartbeat_path {\n185\t                use std::path::Path;\n186\t                // Import the cleanup function from health module\n187\t                // This function has proper error handling: logs, doesn't panic,\n188\t                // and returns Ok(()) even if removal fails (best-effort cleanup).\n189\t                if let Err(e) = crate::health::cleanup_heartbeat_file(Path::new(hb_path)) {\n190\t                    eprintln!(\"Heartbeat cleanup error: {}\", e);\n191\t                } else {\n192\t                    eprintln!(\"Cleaned up heartbeat file: {}\", hb_path);\n193\t                }\n194\t            }\n195\t\n196\t            // Try to write a worker.stopped event to the JSONL log file.\n197\t            // This provides diagnostic information even when the worker is killed abruptly.\n198\t            if let Some(ref log_path) = state.log_file_path {\n199\t                use std::fs::OpenOptions;\n200\t                use std::io::Write;\n201\t\n202\t                let event = serde_json::json!({\n203\t                    \"event_type\": \"worker.stopped\",\n204\t                    \"timestamp\": chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339_opts(chrono::SecondsFormat::Secs, true),\n205\t                    \"data\": {\n206\t                        \"worker_id\": state.worker_name,\n207\t                        \"reason\": \"external_kill\",\n208\t                        \"beads_processed\": state.beads_processed,\n209\t                        \"uptime_secs\": uptime,\n210\t                        \"final_state\": state.last_state,\n211\t                        \"via_atexit_handler\": true\n212\t                    }\n213\t                });\n214\t\n215\t                if let Ok(mut file) = OpenOptions::new().create(true).append(true).open(log_path) {\n216\t                    let _ = writeln!(file, \"{}\", event);\n217\t                    let _ = file.flush();\n218\t                }\n219\t            }\n220\t        }\n221\t    }\n222\t\n223\t    // SAFETY: atexit is safe to call with a function pointer.\n224\t    unsafe {\n225\t        libc::atexit(atexit_handler);\n226\t    }\n227\t}\n228\t\n229\t/// Update the atexit state when the worker state changes.\n230\t///\n231\t/// Called by `set_state` to keep the atexit handler's last state fresh.\n232\tfn update_atexit_state(last_state: String) {\n233\t    if let Some(state) = ATEXIT_WORKER_STATE.lock().unwrap().as_mut() {\n234\t        state.last_state = last_state;\n235\t    }\n236\t}\n237\t\n238\t/// Stub implementations for non-Unix platforms.\n239\t/// These functions are no-ops on platforms where Unix signals are not available.\n240\t#[cfg(not(unix))]\n241\tfn set_global_shutdown_flag(_ptr: usize) {\n242\t    // No-op on non-Unix platforms\n243\t}\n244\t\n245\t#[cfg(not(unix))]\n246\tfn clear_global_shutdown_flag() {\n247\t    // No-op on non-Unix platforms\n248\t}\n249\t\n250\t#[cfg(not(unix))]\n251\tfn register_atexit_handler(\n252\t    _worker_name: String,\n253\t    _beads_processed: u64,\n254\t    _start_time: Instant,\n255\t    _last_state: String,\n256\t    _log_file_path: Option<String>,\n257\t    _heartbeat_path: Option<String>,\n258\t) {\n259\t    // No-op on non-Unix platforms\n260\t}\n261\t\n262\t#[cfg(not(unix))]\n263\tfn update_atexit_state(_last_state: String) {\n264\t    // No-op on non-Unix platforms\n265\t}\n266\t\n267\t/// TTL for race-lost bead exclusions.\n268\t///\n269\t/// After losing a claim race, a bead is excluded from selection for this duration\n270\t/// to prevent infinite loops where the selector returns the same bead repeatedly.\n271\tconst RACE_LOST_EXCLUSION_TTL: Duration = Duration::from_secs(30);\n272\t\n273\t/// Timeout for HANDLING state watchdog.\n274\t///\n275\t/// If the worker remains in HANDLING state for longer than this duration,\n276\t/// the watchdog thread will force a recovery. This is longer than the\n277\t/// inner timeouts (50s, 60s, 90s) to allow normal recovery to work first.\n278\tconst HANDLING_WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT_SECS: u64 = 120;\n279\t\n280\t/// The NEEDLE worker — owns and drives the full state machine.\n281\tpub struct Worker {\n282\t    config: Config,\n283\t    worker_name: String,\n284\t    store: Arc<dyn BeadStore>,\n285\t    /// Home workspace store — kept for restore after processing a remote bead.\n286\t    home_store: Arc<dyn BeadStore>,\n287\t    telemetry: Telemetry,\n288\t    strands: StrandRunner,\n289\t    claimer: Claimer,\n290\t    prompt_builder: PromptBuilder,\n291\t    dispatcher: Dispatcher,\n292\t    outcome_handler: OutcomeHandler,\n293\t    health: HealthMonitor,\n294\t    registry: Registry,\n295\t    rate_limiter: RateLimiter,\n296\t    mitosis_evaluator: MitosisEvaluator,\n297\t\n298\t    // State machine fields\n299\t    state: WorkerState,\n300\t    current_bead: Option<Bead>,\n301\t    exclusion_set: HashSet<BeadId>,\n302\t    /// Race-lost exclusions with TTL — prevents re-selecting beads that just lost a claim race.\n303\t    /// Each entry is (bead_id, expiration_time). Entries are pruned on access.\n304\t    race_lost_exclusions: Vec<(BeadId, Instant)>,\n305\t    /// Beads that lost a claim race in the current selection cycle.\n306\t    /// These are added to exclusion_set to prevent immediate re-selection.\n307\t    /// Cleared at the start of the next SELECTING cycle.\n308\t    race_lost_this_cycle: HashSet<BeadId>,\n309\t    retry_count: u32,\n310\t    consecutive_race_lost: u32,\n311\t    beads_processed: u64,\n312\t    shutdown: Arc<AtomicBool>,\n313\t    last_error: Option<anyhow::Error>,\n314\t    boot_time: Option<Instant>,\n315\t\n316\t    // Transient fields — pass data between state handlers within a single cycle.\n317\t    built_prompt: Option<BuiltPrompt>,\n318\t    current_strand: Option<String>,\n319\t    exec_output: Option<(AgentOutcome, bool)>,\n320\t    /// Effort tracking data for the current bead cycle.\n321\t    last_effort: Option<EffortData>,\n322\t    /// HEAD SHA captured just before agent dispatch; used to detect new commits.\n323\t    pre_dispatch_head: Option<String>,\n324\t    /// The workspace of the current bead store — updated when switching to remote.\n325\t    /// Used to ensure heartbeat reports the actual workspace where work is happening.\n326\t    current_workspace: PathBuf,\n327\t    /// Whether `worker.booting` was already emitted externally (e.g., from CLI layer).\n328\t    /// When true, `run()` skips emitting the booting event to avoid duplicates.\n329\t    booting_emitted: bool,\n330\t    /// Waterfall restart count from the most recent select cycle (for exhausted telemetry).\n331\t    last_waterfall_restarts: u32,\n332\t    /// Names of strands that triggered waterfall restarts in the most recent cycle.\n333\t    last_restart_triggers: Vec<String>,\n334\t    /// Strand evaluations from the most recent select cycle (for exhausted telemetry).\n335\t    last_strand_evaluations: Vec<(String, String, u64)>,\n336\t    /// Timestamp when the worker entered HANDLING state.\n337\t    /// Used by the watchdog to detect stuck HANDLING state.\n338\t    handling_state_entered_at: Option<Instant>,\n339\t    /// Flag set by the watchdog thread when HANDLING state timeout is detected.\n340\t    /// The main worker loop checks this flag and forces recovery if set.\n341\t    watchdog_triggered: Arc<AtomicBool>,\n342\t    /// Handle to the watchdog thread for cleanup on worker drop.\n343\t    #[allow(dead_code)]\n344\t    watchdog_handle: Option<std::thread::JoinHandle<()>>,\n345\t    /// The current bead lifecycle span guard. Created when a bead is claimed,\n346\t    /// dropped when the bead lifecycle ends (after HANDLING or when the bead is released).\n347\t    #[allow(dead_code)]\n348\t    bead_lifecycle_span: Option<tracing::span::EnteredSpan>,\n349\t    /// The last outcome for the current bead (used to record on bead.lifecycle span).\n350\t    last_outcome: Option<String>,\n351\t}\n352\t\n353\timpl Worker {\n354\t    /// Construct a worker using a pre-existing telemetry instance.\n355\t    ///\n356\t    /// Use this when telemetry has already been started (e.g. after emitting\n357\t    /// `worker.booting` from the CLI layer) so that early init steps are\n358\t    /// visible in the JSONL log.\n359\t    pub fn new_with_telemetry(\n360\t        config: Config,\n361\t        worker_name: String,\n362\t        store: Arc<dyn BeadStore>,\n363\t        telemetry: Telemetry,\n364\t    ) -> Self {\n365\t        Self::build(config, worker_name, store, telemetry, true)\n366\t    }\n367\t\n368\t    /// Construct a worker from config, a worker name, and a bead store implementation.\n369\t    ///\n370\t    /// Creates its own telemetry instance. Prefer [`new_with_telemetry`] when\n371\t    /// the caller has already created and started telemetry for early boot\n372\t    /// diagnostics.\n373\t    pub fn new(config: Config, worker_name: String, store: Arc<dyn BeadStore>) -> Self {\n374\t        let qualified_id = format!(\"{}-{}\", config.agent.default, worker_name);\n375\t        let telemetry = Telemetry::from_config(qualified_id.clone(), &config.telemetry)\n376\t            .unwrap_or_else(|e| {\n377\t                tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"failed to create hook-enabled telemetry, falling back\");\n378\t                Telemetry::new(qualified_id.clone())\n379\t            });\n380\t        Self::build(config, worker_name, store, telemetry, false)\n381\t    }\n382\t\n383\t    /// Shared construction logic used by both [`new`] and [`new_with_telemetry`].\n384\t    fn build(\n385\t        config: Config,\n386\t        worker_name: String,\n387\t        store: Arc<dyn BeadStore>,\n388\t        telemetry: Telemetry,\n389\t        booting_emitted: bool,\n390\t    ) -> Self {\n391\t        let qualified_id = format!(\"{}-{}\", config.agent.default, worker_name);\n392\t        let strand_registry = Registry::default_location(&config.workspace.home);\n393\t        let strands =\n394\t            StrandRunner::from_config(&config, &qualified_id, strand_registry, telemetry.clone());\n395\t        let claimer = Claimer::new(\n396\t            store.clone(),\n397\t            std::path::PathBuf::from(\"/tmp\"),\n398\t            config.worker.max_claim_retries,\n399\t            100,\n400\t            telemetry.clone(),\n401\t        );\n402\t        let prompt_builder = PromptBuilder::with_workspace(\n403\t            &config.prompt,\n404\t            &config.workspace.default,\n405\t        )\n406\t        .unwrap_or_else(|e| {\n407\t            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"failed to load workspace learnings, using default prompt builder\");\n408\t            PromptBuilder::new(&config.prompt)\n409\t        })\n410\t        .with_cross_workspace_skills(\n411\t            &config.strands.explore.workspaces,\n412\t            &config.workspace.labels,\n413\t        )\n414\t        .with_global_learnings(&config.strands.learning.global_learnings_file);\n415\t        let dispatcher = match Dispatcher::new(&config, telemetry.clone()) {\n416\t            Ok(d) => d,\n417\t            Err(e) => {\n418\t                tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"failed to load adapters, using built-in defaults\");\n419\t                let builtins = crate::dispatch::builtin_adapters()\n420\t                    .into_iter()\n421\t                    .map(|a| (a.name.clone(), a))\n422\t                    .collect();\n423\t                Dispatcher::with_adapters(builtins, telemetry.clone(), config.agent.timeout)\n424\t            }\n425\t        };\n426\t        let outcome_handler = OutcomeHandler::new(config.clone(), telemetry.clone());\n427\t\n428\t        // Create the shutdown flag BEFORE creating HealthMonitor so we can share it.\n429\t        // This ensures that when the heartbeat emitter's circuit breaker fires,\n430\t        // it sets the worker's shutdown flag (not its own private flag), allowing\n431\t        // the main worker loop to gracefully stop with worker.stopped telemetry.\n432\t        let shutdown = Arc::new(AtomicBool::new(false));\n433\t\n434\t        let health = HealthMonitor::new(\n435\t            config.clone(),\n436\t            worker_name.clone(),\n437\t            telemetry.clone(),\n438\t            Some(shutdown.clone()),\n439\t        );\n440\t        let registry = Registry::default_location(&config.workspace.home);\n441\t        let rate_limiter =\n442\t            RateLimiter::new(config.limits.clone(), &config.workspace.home.join(\"state\"));\n443\t        let mitosis_evaluator = MitosisEvaluator::new(\n444\t            config.strands.mitosis.clone(),\n445\t            telemetry.clone(),\n446\t            std::path::PathBuf::from(\"/tmp\"),\n447\t        );\n448\t\n449\t        // Restore beads_processed from registry if this worker was previously registered\n450\t        // (e.g., hot-reload resume). New workers start at 0.\n451\t        // Match by qualified identity ({adapter}-{worker_id}).\n452\t        let qualified_id = format!(\"{}-{}\", config.agent.default, worker_name);\n453\t        let beads_processed = registry\n454\t            .list()\n455\t            .ok()\n456\t            .and_then(|workers| workers.into_iter().find(|w| w.id == qualified_id))\n457\t            .map(|entry| entry.beads_processed)\n458\t            .unwrap_or(0);\n459\t\n460\t        let default_workspace = config.workspace.default.clone();\n461\t\n462\t        // Create the watchdog trigger flag before creating the Worker.\n463\t        let watchdog_triggered = Arc::new(AtomicBool::new(false));\n464\t\n465\t        Worker {\n466\t            config,\n467\t            worker_name,\n468\t            home_store: store.clone(),\n469\t            store,\n470\t            telemetry,\n471\t            strands,\n472\t            claimer,\n473\t            prompt_builder,\n474\t            dispatcher,\n475\t            outcome_handler,\n476\t            health,\n477\t            registry,\n478\t            rate_limiter,\n479\t            mitosis_evaluator,\n480\t            state: WorkerState::Booting,\n481\t            current_bead: None,\n482\t            exclusion_set: HashSet::new(),\n483\t            race_lost_exclusions: Vec::new(),\n484\t            race_lost_this_cycle: HashSet::new(),\n485\t            retry_count: 0,\n486\t            consecutive_race_lost: 0,\n487\t            beads_processed,\n488\t            shutdown,\n489\t            last_error: None,\n490\t            boot_time: None,\n491\t            built_prompt: None,\n492\t            current_strand: None,\n493\t            exec_output: None,\n494\t            last_effort: None,\n495\t            pre_dispatch_head: None,\n496\t            current_workspace: default_workspace,\n497\t            booting_emitted,\n498\t            last_waterfall_restarts: 0,\n499\t            last_restart_triggers: Vec::new(),\n500\t            last_strand_evaluations: Vec::new(),\n501\t            handling_state_entered_at: None,\n502\t            watchdog_triggered: watchdog_triggered.clone(),\n503\t            watchdog_handle: None,\n504\t            bead_lifecycle_span: None,\n505\t            last_outcome: None,\n506\t        }\n507\t    }\n508\t\n509\t    /// Start the watchdog thread that monitors HANDLING state duration.\n510\t    ///\n511\t    /// The watchdog runs in a separate thread (not part of the Tokio runtime)\n512\t    /// and can detect when the worker is stuck in HANDLING state even if\n513\t    /// the Tokio runtime becomes wedged. If HANDLING state exceeds the\n514\t    /// timeout, the watchdog sets the `watchdog_triggered` flag, which\n515\t    /// the main worker loop checks to force recovery.\n516\t    fn start_watchdog_thread(&mut self) {\n517\t        let watchdog_triggered = self.watchdog_triggered.clone();\n518\t        let handling_state_entered_at_ptr =\n519\t            &self.handling_state_entered_at as *const Option<Instant> as usize;\n520\t\n521\t        let handle = std::thread::spawn(move || {\n522\t            loop {\n523\t                std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_secs(5));\n524\t                if watchdog_triggered.load(Ordering::Relaxed) {\n525\t                    // Watchdog has already triggered, exit the thread.\n526\t                    break;\n527\t                }\n528\t                // Check if we've been in HANDLING state for too long.\n529\t                // We read the timestamp from the Worker struct via the pointer.\n530\t                // SAFETY: The Worker struct outlives the watchdog thread because\n531\t                // the thread is joined when the Worker is dropped.\n532\t                let entered_at = unsafe {\n533\t                    let ptr = handling_state_entered_at_ptr as *const Option<Instant>;\n534\t                    (*ptr).as_ref().copied()\n535\t                };\n536\t\n537\t                if let Some(entry_time) = entered_at {\n538\t                    let elapsed = entry_time.elapsed().as_secs();\n539\t                    if elapsed >= HANDLING_WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT_SECS {\n540\t                        tracing::error!(\n541\t                            elapsed_secs = elapsed,\n542\t                            \"HANDLING state watchdog triggered - forcing recovery\"\n543\t                        );\n544\t                        watchdog_triggered.store(true, Ordering::Release);\n545\t                        break;\n546\t                    }\n547\t                }\n548\t            }\n549\t        });\n550\t\n551\t        self.watchdog_handle = Some(handle);\n552\t    }\n553\t\n554\t    /// Run the worker loop until exhausted, stopped, or errored.\n555\t    ///\n556\t    /// The main loop is a match on `self.state`. Every state has a handler\n557\t    /// that performs its actions and sets `self.state` to the next state.\n558\t    ///\n559\t    /// Guarantees that the telemetry BufWriter is flushed before returning,\n560\t    /// even when the inner state machine exits early via `?`.\n561\t    pub async fn run(&mut self) -> Result<WorkerState> {\n562\t        // Start the telemetry writer now that we are inside the tokio runtime.\n563\t        self.telemetry.start();\n564\t\n565\t        // IMMEDIATE boot event — must be the first thing emitted after telemetry starts.\n566\t        // This ensures we get a trace even if subsequent init steps block indefinitely.\n567\t        // Skip if already emitted externally (e.g., from CLI layer for early boot diagnostics).\n568\t        if !self.booting_emitted {\n569\t            self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::WorkerBooting {\n570\t                worker_name: self.worker_name.clone(),\n571\t                version: env!(\"CARGO_PKG_VERSION\").to_string(),\n572\t            })?;\n573\t            // Force-flush to disk before boot() — if init blocks, we still have a trace.\n574\t            self.telemetry\n575\t                .force_flush_async(std::time::Duration::from_secs(5))\n576\t                .await?;\n577\t        }\n578\t\n579\t        let result = self.run_inner().await;\n580\t\n581\t        // Safety-net flush: shutdown() is idempotent. Normal terminal paths\n582\t        // (stop, handle_exhausted, Errored) already call it; this catches\n583\t        // any early-exit via `?` (boot failure, state handler panic, etc.)\n584\t        // so the BufWriter is always flushed before the tokio Runtime drops.\n585\t        self.telemetry.shutdown().await;\n586\t\n587\t        result\n588\t    }\n589\t\n590\t    /// Inner state machine — called only from [`run()`](Self::run).\n591\t    ///\n592\t    /// May return early via `?` without calling `telemetry.shutdown()`;\n593\t    /// `run()` handles the safety-net flush.\n594\t    async fn run_inner(&mut self) -> Result<WorkerState> {\n595\t        // Boot: validate config and initialize.\n596\t        self.boot()?;\n597\t\n598\t        // Start the watchdog thread that monitors HANDLING state duration.\n599\t        // This must be started after boot() so the Worker struct is fully initialized.\n600\t        self.start_watchdog_thread();\n601\t\n602\t        // Install signal handlers.\n603\t        self.install_signal_handlers();\n604\t\n605\t        // Create the worker.session root span that encompasses the entire worker lifecycle.\n606\t        let worker_id = self.qualified_id();\n607\t        let session_span = tracing::info_span!(\n608\t            \"worker.session\",\n609\t            needle.worker_id = %worker_id,\n610\t            needle.session_id = %self.telemetry.session_id(),\n611\t            needle.agent = %self.config.agent.default,\n612\t            needle.model = %self.config.agent.default, // Will be updated when adapter is resolved\n613\t            needle.workspace = %self.config.workspace.default.display(),\n614\t        );\n615\t        let _enter = session_span.enter();\n616\t\n617\t        loop {\n618\t            // Check for shutdown signal between states.\n619\t            if self.shutdown.load(Ordering::SeqCst) {\n620\t                // Retrieve and clear the last received signal for logging.\n621\t                #[cfg(unix)]\n622\t                let signal_name = {\n623\t                    let sig = LAST_SIGNAL.swap(0, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n624\t                    if sig == 0 {\n625\t                        None\n626\t                    } else {\n627\t                        Some(match sig {\n628\t                            1 => \"SIGHUP\",\n629\t                            2 => \"SIGINT\",\n630\t                            15 => \"SIGTERM\",\n631\t                            _ => \"unknown signal\",\n632\t                        })\n633\t                    }\n634\t                };\n635\t                #[cfg(not(unix))]\n636\t                let signal_name = None;\n637\t\n638\t                let reason = if let Some(name) = signal_name {\n639\t                    format!(\"signal received ({name})\")\n640\t                } else {\n641\t                    \"signal received\".to_string()\n642\t                };\n643\t\n644\t                match self.state {\n645\t                    // If we're in the middle of processing a bead, release it\n646\t                    // before stopping. This ensures the bead is returned to\n647\t                    // the open state and can be claimed by another worker.\n648\t                    WorkerState::Building\n649\t                    | WorkerState::Dispatching\n650\t                    | WorkerState::Executing\n651\t                    | WorkerState::Handling => {\n652\t                        // Release any claimed bead before stopping.\n653\t                        self.release_current_bead(&reason).await;\n654\t                        return self.stop(&reason).await;\n655\t                    }\n656\t                    // For states where we don't hold a bead, stop immediately.\n657\t                    WorkerState::Selecting\n658\t                    | WorkerState::Claiming\n659\t                    | WorkerState::Retrying\n660\t                    | WorkerState::Logging => {\n661\t                        // Release any claimed bead before stopping (should be none in these states).\n662\t                        self.release_current_bead(&reason).await;\n663\t                        return self.stop(&reason).await;\n664\t                    }\n665\t                    WorkerState::Stopped | WorkerState::Exhausted | WorkerState::Errored => {\n666\t                        return self.stop(&reason).await;\n667\t                    }\n668\t                    WorkerState::Booting => {\n669\t                        return self.stop(\"signal received during boot\").await;\n670\t                    }\n671\t                }\n672\t            }\n673\t\n674\t            // Check for watchdog trigger - this indicates HANDLING state is wedged.\n675\t            // The watchdog runs in a separate thread and can detect when the worker\n676\t            // is stuck even if the Tokio runtime becomes unresponsive.\n677\t            if self.watchdog_triggered.load(Ordering::Relaxed)\n678\t                && self.state == WorkerState::Handling\n679\t            {\n680\t                tracing::error!(\"watchdog detected HANDLING state hang, forcing recovery\");\n681\t                // Emit critical timeout event.\n682\t                let bead_id = self.current_bead.as_ref().map(|b| b.id.clone());\n683\t                let _ = self\n684\t                    .telemetry\n685\t                    .emit_try_lock(EventKind::WorkerHandlingTimeout {\n686\t                        bead_id: bead_id.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| BeadId::from(\"unknown\")),\n687\t                        outcome: \"unknown\".to_string(),\n688\t                        operation: \"watchdog\".to_string(),\n689\t                        error: format!(\n690\t                            \"HANDLING state exceeded {}s timeout\",\n691\t                            HANDLING_WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT_SECS\n692\t                        ),\n693\t                    });\n694\t                // Attempt best-effort release if we have a bead.\n695\t                if let Some(ref bead) = self.current_bead {\n696\t                    let bead_id = bead.id.clone();\n697\t                    tracing::warn!(bead_id = %bead_id, \"best-effort bead release due to watchdog timeout\");\n698\t                    let _ =\n699\t                        tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(30), self.store.release(&bead_id))\n700\t                            .await;\n701\t                }\n702\t                // Clear the watchdog trigger and force transition to LOGGING.\n703\t                self.watchdog_triggered.store(false, Ordering::Release);\n704\t                self.handling_state_entered_at = None;\n705\t                // Force transition to LOGGING to recover.\n706\t                self.set_state(WorkerState::Logging)?;\n707\t            }\n708\t\n709\t            match self.state {\n710\t                WorkerState::Selecting => self.do_select().await?,\n711\t                WorkerState::Claiming => self.do_claim().await?,\n712\t                WorkerState::Retrying => self.do_retry().await?,\n713\t                WorkerState::Building => self.do_build().await?,\n714\t                WorkerState::Dispatching => self.do_dispatch().await?,\n715\t                WorkerState::Executing => self.do_execute().await?,\n716\t                WorkerState::Handling => self.do_handle().await?,\n717\t                WorkerState::Logging => self.do_log()?,\n718\t                WorkerState::Exhausted => {\n719\t                    let next = self.handle_exhausted().await?;\n720\t                    match next {\n721\t                        WorkerState::Selecting => continue,\n722\t                        terminal => return Ok(terminal),\n723\t                    }\n724\t                }\n725\t                WorkerState::Stopped => {\n726\t                    return self.stop(\"normal shutdown\").await;\n727\t                }\n728\t                WorkerState::Errored => {\n729\t                    let err = self\n730\t                        .last_error\n731\t                        .take()\n732\t                        .unwrap_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!(\"unknown error\"));\n733\t                    let msg = format!(\"{err}\");\n734\t                    self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::WorkerErrored {\n735\t                        error_type: \"worker_scoped\".to_string(),\n736\t                        error_message: msg.clone(),\n737\t                        beads_processed: self.beads_processed,\n738\t                    })?;\n739\t\n740\t                    // Emit WorkerStopped before exiting so telemetry shows a clean shutdown.\n741\t                    // This ensures operators can distinguish \"exited with error\" from\n742\t                    // \"killed by external agent\" (e.g., SIGKILL, OOM).\n743\t                    let uptime = self.boot_time.map(|t| t.elapsed().as_secs()).unwrap_or(0);\n744\t                    let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::WorkerStopped {\n745\t                        reason: format!(\"error: {msg}\"),\n746\t                        beads_processed: self.beads_processed,\n747\t                        uptime_secs: uptime,\n748\t                    });\n749\t\n750\t                    // Best-effort stop heartbeat on error.\n751\t                    self.health.stop();\n752\t                    // Best-effort deregister on error.\n753\t                    if let Err(e) = self.registry.deregister(&self.qualified_id()) {\n754\t                        tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"failed to deregister from worker registry on error\");\n755\t                    }\n756\t                    self.telemetry.shutdown().await;\n757\t                    return Err(err);\n758\t                }\n759\t                WorkerState::Booting => {\n760\t                    bail!(\"boot() should have transitioned past Booting state\");\n761\t                }\n762\t            }\n763\t        }\n764\t    }\n765\t\n766\t    // ── Boot ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n767\t\n768\t    /// Validate configuration and initialize the worker.\n769\t    ///\n770\t    /// Each step is instrumented with `init.step.started`/`init.step.completed`\n771\t    /// events so that hangs are visible in the telemetry log. Boot duration is\n772\t    /// capped at 60 seconds — if exceeded, the worker self-aborts with a\n773\t    /// `worker.boot.timeout` event and exits with a non-zero code.\n774\t    fn boot(&mut self) -> Result<()> {\n775\t        self.boot_time = Some(Instant::now());\n776\t        const BOOT_TIMEOUT_SECS: u64 = 60;\n777\t\n778\t        // Step: Config validation\n779\t        self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::InitStepStarted {\n780\t            step: \"config_validation\".to_string(),\n781\t        })?;\n782\t        let step_start = Instant::now();\n783\t        let errors = ConfigLoader::validate(&self.config);\n784\t        if !errors.is_empty() {\n785\t            let msg = errors\n786\t                .iter()\n787\t                .map(|e| e.to_string())\n788\t                .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n789\t                .join(\"; \");\n790\t            bail!(\"config validation failed: {msg}\");\n791\t        }\n792\t        self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::InitStepCompleted {\n793\t            step: \"config_validation\".to_string(),\n794\t            duration_ms: step_start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n795\t        })?;\n796\t\n797\t        // Check boot timeout before each step\n798\t        self.check_boot_timeout(BOOT_TIMEOUT_SECS)?;\n799\t\n800\t        // Step: Registry registration\n801\t        self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::InitStepStarted {\n802\t            step: \"registry_registration\".to_string(),\n803\t        })?;\n804\t        let step_start = Instant::now();\n805\t        let qualified_id = format!(\"{}-{}\", self.config.agent.default, self.worker_name);\n806\t        let entry = WorkerEntry {\n807\t            id: qualified_id.clone(),\n808\t            pid: std::process::id(),\n809\t            workspace: self.config.workspace.default.clone(),\n810\t            agent: self.config.agent.default.clone(),\n811\t            model: None,\n812\t            provider: self.resolve_provider(),\n813\t            started_at: chrono::Utc::now(),\n814\t            beads_processed: 0,\n815\t        };\n816\t        if let Err(e) = self.registry.register(entry) {\n817\t            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"failed to register in worker registry\");\n818\t        }\n819\t        self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::InitStepCompleted {\n820\t            step: \"registry_registration\".to_string(),\n821\t            duration_ms: step_start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n822\t        })?;\n823\t\n824\t        // Check boot timeout before each step\n825\t        self.check_boot_timeout(BOOT_TIMEOUT_SECS)?;\n826\t\n827\t        // Step: Idle action validation\n828\t        self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::InitStepStarted {\n829\t            step: \"idle_action_validation\".to_string(),\n830\t        })?;\n831\t        let step_start = Instant::now();\n832\t\n833\t        // Warn if idle_action=exit without supervisor supervision\n834\t        if self.config.worker.idle_action == crate::types::IdleAction::Exit {\n835\t            match self.health.detect_supervisor() {\n836\t                Ok(supervisor_present) => {\n837\t                    if !supervisor_present {\n838\t                        tracing::warn!(\n839\t                            idle_action = \"exit\",\n840\t                            \"no supervisor detected: worker configured to exit when queue is dry will leave orphaned in_progress beads with no reclaim mechanism\"\n841\t                        );\n842\t                        tracing::warn!(\n843\t                            \"to fix: either run workers under a supervisor (needle supervise) or set idle_action=wait in config\"\n844\t                        );\n845\t                    } else {\n846\t                        tracing::info!(\n847\t                            idle_action = \"exit\",\n848\t                            \"supervisor detected: exit policy is safe\"\n849\t                        );\n850\t                    }\n851\t                }\n852\t                Err(e) => {\n853\t                    tracing::debug!(\n854\t                        error = %e,\n855\t                        \"failed to detect supervisor presence, skipping idle_action validation\"\n856\t                    );\n857\t                }\n858\t            }\n859\t        }\n860\t\n861\t        self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::InitStepCompleted {\n862\t            step: \"idle_action_validation\".to_string(),\n863\t            duration_ms: step_start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n864\t        })?;\n865\t\n866\t        // Check boot timeout before each step\n867\t        self.check_boot_timeout(BOOT_TIMEOUT_SECS)?;\n868\t\n869\t        // Step: Heartbeat emitter start\n870\t        self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::InitStepStarted {\n871\t            step: \"heartbeat_emitter\".to_string(),\n872\t        })?;\n873\t        let step_start = Instant::now();\n874\t        if let Err(e) = self.health.start_emitter() {\n875\t            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"failed to start heartbeat emitter\");\n876\t        }\n877\t        self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::InitStepCompleted {\n878\t            step: \"heartbeat_emitter\".to_string(),\n879\t            duration_ms: step_start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n880\t        })?;\n881\t\n882\t        // Emit worker started event — boot complete\n883\t        self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::WorkerStarted {\n884\t            worker_name: self.worker_name.clone(),\n885\t            version: env!(\"CARGO_PKG_VERSION\").to_string(),\n886\t        })?;\n887\t\n888\t        self.set_state(WorkerState::Selecting)?;\n889\t\n890\t        tracing::info!(\n891\t            worker = %self.worker_name,\n892\t            strands = ?self.strands.strand_names(),\n893\t            \"worker booted\"\n894\t        );\n895\t\n896\t        Ok(())\n897\t    }\n898\t\n899\t    /// Check if boot has exceeded the timeout and abort if so.\n900\t    ///\n901\t    /// Emits `worker.boot.timeout` and exits the process with a non-zero\n902\t    /// code. This is a last-resort measure when an init step hangs.\n903\t    fn check_boot_timeout(&self, timeout_secs: u64) -> Result<()> {\n904\t        if let Some(boot_start) = self.boot_time {\n905\t            let elapsed = boot_start.elapsed();\n906\t            if elapsed.as_secs() > timeout_secs {\n907\t                let elapsed_ms = elapsed.as_millis() as u64;\n908\t                // Emit the timeout event before aborting\n909\t                let _ = self\n910\t                    .telemetry\n911\t                    .emit(EventKind::WorkerBootTimeout { elapsed_ms });\n912\t                tracing::error!(\n913\t                    elapsed_ms,\n914\t                    \"boot timeout exceeded {}s — aborting\",\n915\t                    timeout_secs\n916\t                );\n917\t                // Flush telemetry before exit\n918\t                std::mem::forget(self.telemetry.clone());\n919\t                // Exit with a distinct code to indicate boot timeout\n920\t                std::process::exit(71); // EX_OSERR + custom offset\n921\t            }\n922\t        }\n923\t        Ok(())\n924\t    }\n925\t\n926\t    // ── Signal handling ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n927\t\n928\t    /// Install SIGINT, SIGTERM, and SIGHUP handlers that set the shutdown flag.\n929\t    ///\n930\t    /// SIGHUP is handled because when the parent bash dies (e.g., tmux session\n931\t    /// killed, external reaper), the child process receives SIGHUP by default.\n932\t    /// Without a handler, the process terminates immediately without emitting\n933\t    /// worker.stopped telemetry or flushing the telemetry buffer.\n934\t    ///\n935\t    /// Uses synchronous signal handlers via libc/signal-hook to ensure signals\n936\t    /// are caught immediately, even if the tokio runtime hasn't polled async\n937\t    /// signal tasks yet. This prevents silent process termination when signals\n938\t    /// arrive early (e.g., SIGHUP from parent bash death during worker startup).\n939\t    fn install_signal_handlers(&self) {\n940\t        // Store a global reference to the shutdown flag for signal handlers.\n941\t        // We use a leak to ensure the reference lives for the entire program duration.\n942\t        let shutdown_ptr = Arc::into_raw(self.shutdown.clone()) as usize;\n943\t        set_global_shutdown_flag(shutdown_ptr);\n944\t\n945\t        // Register atexit handler to emit worker.stopped telemetry on unexpected termination.\n946\t        // This provides diagnostic information when the worker is killed by an external\n947\t        // process (e.g., capacity governor, OOM killer, SIGKILL).\n948\t        let start_time = self.boot_time.unwrap_or_else(Instant::now);\n949\t        let heartbeat_path = self.health.heartbeat_path().to_str().map(String::from);\n950\t        register_atexit_handler(\n951\t            self.worker_name.clone(),\n952\t            self.beads_processed,\n953\t            start_time,\n954\t            format!(\"{:?}\", self.state),\n955\t            None, // log_file_path - not available during boot\n956\t            heartbeat_path,\n957\t        );\n958\t\n959\t        #[cfg(unix)]\n960\t        {\n961\t            // Install synchronous signal handlers using libc.\n962\t            // These handlers are called immediately when the signal is received,\n963\t            // before the tokio runtime has a chance to process any async tasks.\n964\t            unsafe {\n965\t                install_unix_signal_handlers();\n966\t            }\n967\t        }\n968\t\n969\t        #[cfg(not(unix))]\n970\t        {\n971\t            // On non-Unix platforms, use tokio's ctrl_c handler.\n972\t            let shutdown_int = self.shutdown.clone();\n973\t            tokio::spawn(async move {\n974\t                if tokio::signal::ctrl_c().await.is_ok() {\n975\t                    tracing::info!(\"received SIGINT, initiating graceful shutdown\");\n976\t                    shutdown_int.store(true, Ordering::SeqCst);\n977\t                }\n978\t            });\n979\t        }\n980\t    }\n981\t\n982\t    // ── State handlers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n983\t\n984\t    /// SELECTING: run strand waterfall to find a candidate bead.\n985\t    async fn do_select(&mut self) -> Result<()> {\n986\t        // Clear per-cycle state.\n987\t        // Preserve race-lost exclusions with TTL and beads that lost a race in the current cycle.\n988\t        // NOTE: Do NOT reset retry_count or consecutive_race_lost here — they must\n989\t        // accumulate across cycles to prevent infinite race-lost loops (see needle-aad8).\n990\t        self.race_lost_this_cycle.clear();\n991\t        self.current_bead = None;\n992\t        self.current_strand = None;\n993\t\n994\t        // Restore home store if it was swapped for a remote workspace.\n995\t        self.restore_home_store();\n996\t\n997\t        // Update heartbeat with home workspace (not current_workspace which\n998\t        // might be stale). This ensures heartbeat reports correctly even if\n999\t        // restore_home_store() was a no-op (stores already equal).\n1000\t        self.health.update_state(\n1001\t            &WorkerState::Selecting,\n1002\t            None,\n1003\t            Some(self.config.workspace.default.as_path()),\n1004\t        );\n1005\t\n1006\t        // Try atomic claim_auto first (server-selected bead in one transaction).\n1007\t        // This eliminates the race condition where two workers both see the same\n1008\t        // bead in ready() and race to claim it.\n1009\t        let strand = \"auto\";\n1010\t        let claim = self.claimer.claim_auto(&self.qualified_id(), strand).await;\n1011\t\n1012\t        match claim {\n1013\t            Ok(ClaimResult::Claimed(bead)) => {\n1014\t                tracing::info!(bead_id = %bead.id, \"atomically claimed bead via claim_auto\");\n1015\t                self.current_bead = Some(bead);\n1016\t                self.current_strand = Some(strand.to_string());\n1017\t                self.consecutive_race_lost = 0;\n1018\t                self.set_state(WorkerState::Building)?;\n1019\t                return Ok(());\n1020\t            }\n1021\t            Ok(ClaimResult::NotClaimable { reason }) => {\n1022\t                tracing::debug!(\n1023\t                    reason,\n1024\t                    \"claim_auto returned no beads, falling back to strand waterfall\"\n1025\t                );\n1026\t                // Fall through to strand waterfall\n1027\t            }\n1028\t            Err(e) => {\n1029\t                tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"claim_auto failed, falling back to strand waterfall\");\n1030\t                // Fall through to strand waterfall\n1031\t            }\n1032\t            Ok(other) => {\n1033\t                tracing::warn!(\n1034\t                    ?other,\n1035\t                    \"claim_auto returned unexpected result, falling back to strand waterfall\"\n1036\t                );\n1037\t                // Fall through to strand waterfall\n1038\t            }\n1039\t        }\n1040\t\n1041\t        // Fallback: run strand waterfall to find a candidate bead.\n1042\t        let exclusions = self.current_exclusions();\n1043\t        let candidate = self\n1044\t            .strands\n1045\t            .select(self.store.as_ref(), &exclusions)\n1046\t            .await?;\n1047\t        self.last_waterfall_restarts = candidate.waterfall_restarts;\n1048\t        self.last_restart_triggers = candidate.restart_triggers.clone();\n1049\t        self.last_strand_evaluations = candidate\n1050\t            .strand_evaluations\n1051\t            .iter()\n1052\t            .map(|e| (e.strand_name.clone(), e.result.clone(), e.duration_ms))\n1053\t            .collect();\n1054\t\n1055\t        match candidate.bead {\n1056\t            Some((bead, strand_name)) => {\n1057\t                tracing::debug!(bead_id = %bead.id, strand = %strand_name, \"candidate found\");\n1058\t\n1059\t                // If the bead is from a remote workspace (found by Explore),\n1060\t                // swap the active store so claim/show/release operate on the\n1061\t                // correct workspace.
