Only switch if the workspace has a real\n1062\t                // .beads/ directory — avoids false triggers from mock/stub beads.\n1063\t                let bead_ws = bead.workspace.clone();\n1064\t                if !is_workspace_unset(&bead_ws)\n1065\t                    && bead_ws != self.config.workspace.default\n1066\t                    && bead_ws.join(\".beads\").is_dir()\n1067\t                {\n1068\t                    tracing::info!(\n1069\t                        bead_id = %bead.id,\n1070\t                        remote_workspace = %bead_ws.display(),\n1071\t                        \"bead is from remote workspace, switching store\"\n1072\t                    );\n1073\t                    self.switch_store_to(&bead_ws)?;\n1074\t                }\n1075\t\n1076\t                // Always update current_workspace to reflect the bead's workspace.\n1077\t                // For local beads, this keeps heartbeat consistent with home workspace.\n1078\t                // For cross-workspace beads, this ensures heartbeat reports where\n1079\t                // the work is actually happening.\n1080\t                if !is_workspace_unset(&bead_ws) {\n1081\t                    self.current_workspace = bead_ws.clone();\n1082\t                }\n1083\t\n1084\t                self.current_bead = Some(bead);\n1085\t                self.current_strand = Some(strand_name);\n1086\t\n1087\t                // Update heartbeat immediately with the bead's workspace so that\n1088\t                // observers see the correct workspace even before transitioning to\n1089\t                // CLAIMING. This ensures heartbeats are accurate for cross-workspace\n1090\t                // work (see bead needle-c63c).\n1091\t                self.health.update_state(\n1092\t                    &WorkerState::Selecting,\n1093\t                    Some(&self.current_bead.as_ref().unwrap().id),\n1094\t                    Some(&bead_ws),\n1095\t                );\n1096\t\n1097\t                self.set_state(WorkerState::Claiming)?;\n1098\t            }\n1099\t            None => {\n1100\t                self.set_state(WorkerState::Exhausted)?;\n1101\t            }\n1102\t        }\n1103\t\n1104\t        Ok(())\n1105\t    }\n1106\t\n1107\t    /// Swap the active bead store to a remote workspace.\n1108\t    ///\n1109\t    /// Creates a new BrCliBeadStore and rebuilds the Claimer to use it.\n1110\t    /// The home store is restored at the start of the next select cycle.\n1111\t    fn switch_store_to(&mut self, workspace: &std::path::Path) -> Result<()> {\n1112\t        let remote_store = Arc::new(\n1113\t            crate::bead_store::BrCliBeadStore::discover(workspace.to_path_buf())\n1114\t                .context(\"failed to create bead store for remote workspace\")?,\n1115\t        );\n1116\t        self.store = remote_store.clone();\n1117\t        self.current_workspace = workspace.to_path_buf();\n1118\t        self.claimer = Claimer::new(\n1119\t            remote_store,\n1120\t            std::path::PathBuf::from(\"/tmp\"),\n1121\t            self.config.worker.max_claim_retries,\n1122\t            100,\n1123\t            self.telemetry.clone(),\n1124\t        );\n1125\t        // Update registry so observers see the actual workspace being processed.\n1126\t        if let Err(e) = self\n1127\t            .registry\n1128\t            .update_workspace(&self.qualified_id(), workspace)\n1129\t        {\n1130\t            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"failed to update registry workspace\");\n1131\t        }\n1132\t        Ok(())\n1133\t    }\n1134\t\n1135\t    /// Restore the home workspace store if it was swapped for a remote bead.\n1136\t    fn restore_home_store(&mut self) {\n1137\t        if !Arc::ptr_eq(&self.store, &self.home_store) {\n1138\t            tracing::debug!(\"restoring home workspace store\");\n1139\t            self.store = self.home_store.clone();\n1140\t            self.current_workspace = self.config.workspace.default.clone();\n1141\t            self.claimer = Claimer::new(\n1142\t                self.home_store.clone(),\n1143\t                std::path::PathBuf::from(\"/tmp\"),\n1144\t                self.config.worker.max_claim_retries,\n1145\t                100,\n1146\t                self.telemetry.clone(),\n1147\t            );\n1148\t            // Update registry to reflect return to home workspace.\n1149\t            if let Err(e) = self\n1150\t                .registry\n1151\t                .update_workspace(&self.qualified_id(), &self.config.workspace.default)\n1152\t            {\n1153\t                tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"failed to update registry workspace\");\n1154\t            }\n1155\t        }\n1156\t    }\n1157\t\n1158\t    /// CLAIMING: attempt to claim the selected bead.\n1159\t    async fn do_claim(&mut self) -> Result<()> {\n1160\t        let bead_id = match self.current_bead {\n1161\t            Some(ref b) => b.id.clone(),\n1162\t            None => {\n1163\t                self.set_state(WorkerState::Selecting)?;\n1164\t                return Ok(());\n1165\t            }\n1166\t        };\n1167\t\n1168\t        // Build the current exclusion set and pass it to claim_one. This\n1169\t        // prevents claim_one from attempting to claim a bead that was\n1170\t        // just race-lost (which would cause a tight loop).\n1171\t        let exclusions = self.current_exclusions();\n1172\t        let strand = self.current_strand.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"unknown\");\n1173\t\n1174\t        // Create the bead.claim span that wraps the claim operation.\n1175\t        // Note: This span is a child of strand.{name}, not bead.lifecycle,\n1176\t        // because bead.lifecycle is only created after the claim succeeds.\n1177\t        let claim_span = tracing::info_span!(\n1178\t            \"bead.claim\",\n1179\t            needle.bead.id = %bead_id.as_ref(),\n1180\t            needle.claim.retry_number = tracing::field::Empty,\n1181\t            needle.claim.result = tracing::field::Empty,\n1182\t        );\n1183\t        let _claim_enter = claim_span.enter();\n1184\t\n1185\t        // Record the initial retry number (will be updated by claim module)\n1186\t        tracing::Span::current().record(\"needle.claim.retry_number\", 1u32);\n1187\t\n1188\t        let claim = self\n1189\t            .claimer\n1190\t            .claim_one(&bead_id, &self.qualified_id(), &exclusions, Some(strand))\n1191\t            .await?;\n1192\t\n1193\t        match claim {\n1194\t            ClaimResult::Claimed(mut bead) => {\n1195\t                tracing::info!(bead_id = %bead.id, title = %bead.title, \"claimed bead\");\n1196\t                self.consecutive_race_lost = 0;\n1197\t                self.retry_count = 0;\n1198\t                self.clear_all_exclusions();\n1199\t                // Preserve the workspace from the pre-claim bead (set by\n1200\t                // Explore for remote beads). The claimed bead from br's JSON\n1201\t                // returns source_repo as \".\" (cwd-relative), so we treat empty\n1202\t                // or \".\" as unset and restore from the pre-claim bead.\n1203\t                if is_workspace_unset(&bead.workspace) {\n1204\t                    if let Some(ref pre_claim) = self.current_bead {\n1205\t                        if !is_workspace_unset(&pre_claim.workspace) {\n1206\t                            bead.workspace = pre_claim.workspace.clone();\n1207\t                        }\n1208\t                    }\n1209\t                }\n1210\t                self.current_bead = Some(bead);\n1211\t                // Start effort tracking for this cycle.\n1212\t                self.last_effort = Some(EffortData {\n1213\t                    cycle_start: Instant::now(),\n1214\t                    agent_name: String::new(),\n1215\t                    model: None,\n1216\t                    tokens: dispatch::TokenUsage::default(),\n1217\t                    estimated_cost_usd: None,\n1218\t                });\n1219\t\n1220\t                // Compute bead metadata for the lifecycle span.\n1221\t                let bead_priority = self.current_bead.as_ref().map(|b| b.priority);\n1222\t                let bead_title_hash = self.current_bead.as_ref().map(|b| {\n1223\t                    use std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher;\n1224\t                    use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n1225\t                    let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();\n1226\t                    b.title.hash(&mut hasher);\n1227\t                    format!(\"{:x}\", hasher.finish())\n1228\t                });\n1229\t\n1230\t                // Create the bead.lifecycle span after the claim succeeds.\n1231\t                // This span will remain active for the entire bead processing.\n1232\t                let lifecycle_span = tracing::info_span!(\n1233\t                    \"bead.lifecycle\",\n1234\t                    needle.bead.id = %self.current_bead.as_ref().map(|b| b.id.as_ref()).unwrap_or(\"unknown\"),\n1235\t                    needle.bead.priority = bead_priority.unwrap_or(0),\n1236\t                    needle.bead.title_hash = %bead_title_hash.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"unknown\"),\n1237\t                    needle.bead.outcome = tracing::field::Empty, // Will be set on completion\n1238\t                );\n1239\t                self.bead_lifecycle_span = Some(lifecycle_span.entered());\n1240\t\n1241\t                // Note: The claim_span (_claim_enter) is dropped here, closing the bead.claim span.\n1242\t                // The bead.lifecycle span is now active and will be the parent for subsequent operations.\n1243\t\n1244\t                self.set_state(WorkerState::Building)?;\n1245\t            }\n1246\t            ClaimResult::RaceLost { claimed_by } => {\n1247\t                tracing::debug!(bead_id = %bead_id, %claimed_by, \"claim race lost\");\n1248\t                // Set the claim span result attribute\n1249\t                tracing::Span::current().record(\"needle.claim.result\", \"race_lost\");\n1250\t                // Set Error status on the claim span\n1251\t                tracing::Span::current().record(\"otel.status_code\", 2u64);\n1252\t                tracing::Span::current().record(\"otel.status_description\", \"race_lost\");\n1253\t                // Add to race-lost exclusions with TTL (persists across cycles)\n1254\t                let expires = Instant::now() + RACE_LOST_EXCLUSION_TTL;\n1255\t                self.race_lost_exclusions.push((bead_id.clone(), expires));\n1256\t                // Also add to exclusion_set for immediate protection in the current cycle\n1257\t                self.exclusion_set.insert(bead_id.clone());\n1258\t                self.race_lost_this_cycle.insert(bead_id);\n1259\t                self.retry_count += 1;\n1260\t                self.consecutive_race_lost += 1;\n1261\t                // The claim_span is dropped here, closing the bead.claim span.\n1262\t                self.set_state(WorkerState::Retrying)?;\n1263\t            }\n1264\t            ClaimResult::NotClaimable { reason } => {\n1265\t                tracing::debug!(bead_id = %bead_id, %reason, \"bead not claimable\");\n1266\t                // Set the claim span result attribute\n1267\t                tracing::Span::current().record(\"needle.claim.result\", &reason);\n1268\t                // Set Error status on the claim span\n1269\t                tracing::Span::current().record(\"otel.status_code\", 2u64);\n1270\t                tracing::Span::current().record(\"otel.status_description\", &reason);\n1271\t                self.consecutive_race_lost = 0;\n1272\t                self.exclusion_set.insert(bead_id);\n1273\t                self.current_bead = None;\n1274\t                // The claim_span is dropped here, closing the bead.claim span.\n1275\t                self.set_state(WorkerState::Selecting)?;\n1276\t            }\n1277\t        }\n1278\t\n1279\t        Ok(())\n1280\t    }\n1281\t\n1282\t    /// RETRYING: decide whether to retry claiming or move on.\n1283\t    ///\n1284\t    /// Tracks consecutive race_lost across retry cycles. When the count\n1285\t    /// exceeds `claim_race_lost_skip`, the worker treats the ready queue\n1286\t    /// as effectively empty and transitions to Exhausted instead of\n1287\t    /// spinning indefinitely on the same bead.\n1288\t    async fn do_retry(&mut self) -> Result<()> {\n1289\t        let skip_threshold = self.config.worker.claim_race_lost_skip;\n1290\t\n1291\t        if self.consecutive_race_lost >= skip_threshold {\n1292\t            tracing::warn!(\n1293\t                consecutive_race_lost = self.consecutive_race_lost,\n1294\t                threshold = skip_threshold,\n1295\t                \"consecutive race_lost exceeded skip threshold, treating queue as exhausted\"\n1296\t            );\n1297\t            self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::ClaimRaceLostSkipped {\n1298\t                consecutive_losses: self.consecutive_race_lost,\n1299\t                threshold: skip_threshold,\n1300\t            })?;\n1301\t            self.consecutive_race_lost = 0;\n1302\t            self.retry_count = 0;\n1303\t            self.exclusion_set.clear();\n1304\t            self.race_lost_this_cycle.clear();\n1305\t            self.current_bead = None;\n1306\t            self.set_state(WorkerState::Exhausted)?;\n1307\t            return Ok(());\n1308\t        }\n1309\t\n1310\t        // Exponential backoff: start at 100ms, doubling each time, capped at 5s.\n1311\t        // This ensures even the first retry has a small delay to prevent tight loops.\n1312\t        let backoff_ms = if self.consecutive_race_lost > 0 {\n1313\t            // For race-lost retries: 100ms, 200ms, 400ms, 800ms, 1600ms, 3200ms, 5000ms (capped)\n1314\t            std::cmp::min(\n1315\t                100 * (1u64 << (self.consecutive_race_lost - 1).min(5)),\n1316\t                5000,\n1317\t            )\n1318\t        } else {\n1319\t            // For other retries (e.g., max_claim_retries): 100ms minimum\n1320\t            100\n1321\t        };\n1322\t        tracing::debug!(\n1323\t            consecutive_race_lost = self.consecutive_race_lost,\n1324\t            backoff_ms,\n1325\t            \"backing off before retry\"\n1326\t        );\n1327\t        tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(backoff_ms)).await;\n1328\t\n1329\t        if self.retry_count < self.config.worker.max_claim_retries {\n1330\t            self.set_state(WorkerState::Selecting)?;\n1331\t        } else {\n1332\t            tracing::debug!(\n1333\t                retry_count = self.retry_count,\n1334\t                \"max claim retries exceeded, clearing retry state for next cycle\"\n1335\t            );\n1336\t            self.retry_count = 0;\n1337\t            self.consecutive_race_lost = 0;\n1338\t            self.exclusion_set.clear();\n1339\t            self.race_lost_this_cycle.clear();\n1340\t            // NOTE: Do NOT clear race_lost_exclusions here. Those have TTL-based\n1341\t            // expiration and must persist to prevent re-selecting the same bead\n1342\t            // that just lost a claim race. Clearing them would cause an infinite\n1343\t            // race-lost loop (see needle-aad8).\n1344\t            self.current_bead = None;\n1345\t            self.set_state(WorkerState::Selecting)?;\n1346\t        }\n1347\t        Ok(())\n1348\t    }\n1349\t\n1350\t    /// BUILDING: construct prompt from claimed bead.\n1351\t    async fn do_build(&mut self) -> Result<()> {\n1352\t        let bead = match self.current_bead {\n1353\t            Some(ref b) => b.clone(),\n1354\t            None => {\n1355\t                bail!(\"BUILDING state without current_bead — invariant violated\");\n1356\t            }\n1357\t        };\n1358\t\n1359\t        let build_ws = if is_workspace_unset(&bead.workspace) {\n1360\t            self.config.workspace.default.clone()\n1361\t        } else {\n1362\t            bead.workspace.clone()\n1363\t        };\n1364\t\n1365\t        let worker_name = self.worker_name.clone();\n1366\t        let prompt_builder = self.prompt_builder.clone();\n1367\t\n1368\t        // Wrap prompt building in timeout. The build operation can be slow for\n1369\t        // large workspaces with many learning files.\n1370\t        // Enforce minimum timeout to prevent indefinite hangs (issue needle-3igr).\n1371\t        const MIN_BUILDING_TIMEOUT_SECS: u64 = 60;\n1372\t        let timeout_secs = self\n1373\t            .config\n1374\t            .worker\n1375\t            .building_timeout\n1376\t            .max(MIN_BUILDING_TIMEOUT_SECS);\n1377\t        let timeout_dur = std::time::Duration::from_secs(timeout_secs);\n1378\t        let bead_id = bead.id.clone();\n1379\t        let heartbeat_bead_id = bead_id.clone();\n1380\t        let telemetry = self.telemetry.clone();\n1381\t\n1382\t        // Enter the bead.prompt_build span for the prompt building phase.\n1383\t        let prompt_build_span = tracing::info_span!(\n1384\t            \"bead.prompt_build\",\n1385\t            needle.bead.id = %bead_id,\n1386\t        );\n1387\t        let _prompt_build_enter = prompt_build_span.enter();\n1388\t\n1389\t        // Spawn heartbeat task that emits periodic updates during the build.\n1390\t        // Heartbeat interval: every 30 seconds.\n1391\t        let heartbeat_handle = tokio::spawn(async move {\n1392\t            let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(std::time::Duration::from_secs(30));\n1393\t            let start = std::time::Instant::now();\n1394\t            loop {\n1395\t                interval.tick().await;\n1396\t                let elapsed_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n1397\t                let _ = telemetry.emit(EventKind::BuildHeartbeat {\n1398\t                    bead_id: heartbeat_bead_id.clone(),\n1399\t                    elapsed_ms,\n1400\t                });\n1401\t            }\n1402\t        });\n1403\t\n1404\t        // Check if we should use SPLIT mode instead of normal PLUCK.\n1405\t        // Auto-split triggers when a bead has >= split_after_failures consecutive failures.\n1406\t        let (template_name, failure_count) = self.check_split_mode(&bead).await;\n1407\t\n1408\t        let mut prompt = match tokio::time::timeout(\n1409\t            timeout_dur,\n1410\t            tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || {\n1411\t                if template_name == \"split\" {\n1412\t                    prompt_builder.build_split(&bead, &build_ws, &worker_name, failure_count)\n1413\t                } else {\n1414\t                    prompt_builder.build_pluck(&bead, &build_ws, &worker_name)\n1415\t                }\n1416\t            }),\n1417\t        )\n1418\t        .await\n1419\t        {\n1420\t            Ok(Ok(result)) => result?,\n1421\t            Ok(Err(e)) => {\n1422\t                heartbeat_handle.abort();\n1423\t                bail!(\"prompt building task failed: {}\", e);\n1424\t            }\n1425\t            Err(_) => {\n1426\t                heartbeat_handle.abort();\n1427\t                // Timeout: release the bead and transition to RETRYING.\n1428\t                tracing::error!(\n1429\t                    bead_id = %bead_id,\n1430\t                    timeout_secs = timeout_secs,\n1431\t                    configured_timeout = self.config.worker.building_timeout,\n1432\t                    \"BUILDING state timed out\"\n1433\t                );\n1434\t\n1435\t                // Set Error status on the bead.prompt_build span\n1436\t                tracing::Span::current().record(\"otel.status_code\", 2u64);\n1437\t                tracing::Span::current().record(\n1438\t                    \"otel.status_description\",\n1439\t                    format!(\"timeout after {}s\", timeout_secs),\n1440\t                );\n1441\t\n1442\t                // Emit build.timeout event.\n1443\t                let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::BuildTimeout {\n1444\t                    bead_id: bead_id.clone(),\n1445\t                    timeout_secs,\n1446\t                });\n1447\t\n1448\t                // Release the bead with timeout protection.\n1449\t                let _ = tokio::time::timeout(\n1450\t                    std::time::Duration::from_secs(30),\n1451\t                    self.store.release(&bead_id),\n1452\t                )\n1453\t                .await;\n1454\t\n1455\t                // Emit bead.released event.\n1456\t                let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::BeadReleased {\n1457\t                    bead_id: bead_id.clone(),\n1458\t                    reason: \"build_timeout\".to_string(),\n1459\t                });\n1460\t\n1461\t                // Clear current bead and transition to RETRYING.\n1462\t                self.current_bead = None;\n1463\t                self.set_state(WorkerState::Retrying)?;\n1464\t                return Ok(());\n1465\t            }\n1466\t        };\n1467\t\n1468\t        // Stop heartbeat task.\n1469\t        heartbeat_handle.abort();\n1470\t\n1471\t        // Prepend the HOOP dispatch tag so session tailers can join transcripts\n1472\t        // back to beads. Format: [needle:<qualified-worker>:<bead-id>:<strand>]\n1473\t        let strand = self.current_strand.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"pluck\");\n1474\t        prompt.content = format!(\n1475\t            \"[needle:{}:{}:{}]\\n{}\",\n1476\t            self.qualified_id(),\n1477\t            bead_id,\n1478\t            strand,\n1479\t            prompt.content\n1480\t        );\n1481\t\n1482\t        // Store the prompt for the dispatch phase. We use a transient field pattern:\n1483\t        // the prompt is passed via self.built_prompt.\n1484\t        self.built_prompt = Some(prompt);\n1485\t        self.set_state(WorkerState::Dispatching)?;\n1486\t        Ok(())\n1487\t    }\n1488\t\n1489\t    /// DISPATCHING: check rate limits, resolve adapter, and prepare for execution.\n1490\t    async fn do_dispatch(&mut self) -> Result<()> {\n1491\t        if self.current_bead.is_none() {\n1492\t            bail!(\"DISPATCHING state without current_bead — invariant violated\");\n1493\t        }\n1494\t\n1495\t        // Check rate limits before dispatching.\n1496\t        let adapter = self.resolve_adapter()?;\n1497\t        let provider = adapter.provider.as_deref();\n1498\t        let model = adapter.model.as_deref();\n1499\t\n1500\t        // Enter the agent.dispatch span for the dispatching phase.\n1501\t        let _bead_id = self.current_bead.as_ref().map(|b| b.id.clone());\n1502\t        let dispatch_span = tracing::info_span!(\n1503\t            \"agent.dispatch\",\n1504\t            gen_ai.system = %provider.unwrap_or(\"unknown\"),\n1505\t            gen_ai.request.model = %model.unwrap_or(\"unknown\"),\n1506\t            needle.agent.pid = tracing::field::Empty, // Will be set when process starts\n1507\t            needle.agent.exit_code = tracing::field::Empty, // Will be set after execution\n1508\t        );\n1509\t        let _dispatch_enter = dispatch_span.enter();\n1510\t\n1511\t        let decision = self.rate_limiter.check(provider, model, &self.registry)?;\n1512\t\n1513\t        if !decision.is_allowed() {\n1514\t            let reason = format!(\"{decision}\");\n1515\t            tracing::info!(\n1516\t                reason = %reason,\n1517\t                \"rate limited, waiting before retry\"\n1518\t            );\n1519\t            self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::RateLimitWait {\n1520\t                provider: provider.unwrap_or(\"unknown\").to_string(),\n1521\t                model: model.map(|s| s.to_string()),\n1522\t                reason: reason.clone(),\n1523\t            })?;\n1524\t\n1525\t            // Wait before retrying (5 seconds).\n1526\t            tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_secs(5)).await;\n1527\t\n1528\t            // Stay in Dispatching state to retry the rate limit check.\n1529\t            return Ok(());\n1530\t        }\n1531\t\n1532\t        self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::RateLimitAllowed {\n1533\t            provider: provider.unwrap_or(\"unknown\").to_string(),\n1534\t            model: model.map(|s| s.to_string()),\n1535\t        })?;\n1536\t\n1537\t        // Check system resources (CPU and memory warnings).\n1538\t        crate::rate_limit::RateLimiter::check_system_resources(\n1539\t            self.config.worker.cpu_load_warn,\n1540\t            self.config.worker.memory_free_warn_mb,\n1541\t            &self.telemetry,\n1542\t        );\n1543\t\n1544\t        self.set_state(WorkerState::Executing)?;\n1545\t        Ok(())\n1546\t    }\n1547\t\n1548\t    /// EXECUTING: run the agent process and capture output.\n1549\t    async fn do_execute(&mut self) -> Result<()> {\n1550\t        let bead = match self.current_bead {\n1551\t            Some(ref b) => b.clone(),\n1552\t            None => {\n1553\t                bail!(\"EXECUTING state without current_bead — invariant violated\");\n1554\t            }\n1555\t        };\n1556\t\n1557\t        let prompt = match self.built_prompt.take() {\n1558\t            Some(p) => p,\n1559\t            None => {\n1560\t                bail!(\"EXECUTING state without built_prompt — invariant violated\");\n1561\t            }\n1562\t        };\n1563\t\n1564\t        let adapter = self.resolve_adapter()?;\n1565\t\n1566\t        // Race the dispatch against the shutdown signal.\n1567\t        let was_interrupted;\n1568\t        let exec_result = if self.shutdown.load(Ordering::SeqCst) {\n1569\t            // Already shutting down — don't start the agent.\n1570\t            was_interrupted = true;\n1571\t            None\n1572\t        } else {\n1573\t            // Use the bead's workspace if set (remote bead from Explore),\n1574\t            // otherwise fall back to the config's default workspace.\n1575\t            let dispatch_ws = if is_workspace_unset(&bead.workspace) {\n1576\t                &self.config.workspace.default\n1577\t            } else {\n1578\t                &bead.workspace\n1579\t            };\n1580\t            // Capture HEAD so do_handle can tag new commits with Bead-Id on success.\n1581\t            // Wrap in timeout to prevent indefinite hang if git subprocess hangs.\n1582\t            match tokio::time::timeout(\n1583\t                std::time::Duration::from_secs(10),\n1584\t                commit_hook::git_head(dispatch_ws.to_str().unwrap_or(\".\")),\n1585\t            )\n1586\t            .await\n1587\t            {\n1588\t                Ok(Ok(head)) => {\n1589\t                    self.pre_dispatch_head = Some(head);\n1590\t                }\n1591\t                Ok(Err(e)) => {\n1592\t                    tracing::debug!(\n1593\t                        workspace = %dispatch_ws.display(),\n1594\t                        error = %e,\n1595\t                        \"git_head failed (not a git repo or git error), skipping Bead-Id trailer\"\n1596\t                    );\n1597\t                }\n1598\t                Err(_) => {\n1599\t                    tracing::warn!(\n1600\t                        workspace = %dispatch_ws.display(),\n1601\t                        \"git_head timed out after 10s, skipping Bead-Id trailer\"\n1602\t                    );\n1603\t                }\n1604\t            }\n1605\t            // Enter the agent.execution span for the actual agent process execution.\n1606\t            // This is a child of agent.dispatch.\n1607\t            // We need to record attributes on the parent agent.dispatch span after execution,\n1608\t            // so we use a scope to drop the execution_span guard first.\n1609\t            let (result, exec_tokens) = {\n1610\t                let execution_span = tracing::info_span!(\n1611\t                    \"agent.execution\",\n1612\t                    needle.bead.id = %bead.id,\n1613\t                );\n1614\t                let _execution_enter = execution_span.enter();\n1615\t\n1616\t                let result = self\n1617\t                    .dispatcher\n1618\t                    .dispatch(&bead.id, &prompt, &adapter, dispatch_ws)\n1619\t                    .await?;\n1620\t\n1621\t                // Set span status based on exit code: 0 = Ok, non-zero = Error\n1622\t                if result.exit_code != 0 {\n1623\t                    tracing::Span::current().record(\"otel.status_code\", 2u64);\n1624\t                    tracing::Span::current().record(\n1625\t                        \"otel.status_description\",\n1626\t                        format!(\"exit_code: {}\", result.exit_code),\n1627\t                    );\n1628\t                }\n1629\t\n1630\t                // Extract tokens from the result while still in the execution span.\n1631\t                let exec_tokens = dispatch::extract_tokens(\n1632\t                    &adapter.token_extraction,\n1633\t                    &result.stdout,\n1634\t                    &result.stderr,\n1635\t                );\n1636\t                (result, exec_tokens)\n1637\t            };\n1638\t\n1639\t            // Now we're back in the agent.dispatch span. Record the execution results.\n1640\t            tracing::Span::current().record(\"needle.agent.pid\", result.pid);\n1641\t            tracing::Span::current().record(\"needle.agent.exit_code\", result.exit_code);\n1642\t            if let Some(input_tokens) = exec_tokens.input_tokens {\n1643\t                tracing::Span::current().record(\"gen_ai.usage.input_tokens\", input_tokens);\n1644\t            }\n1645\t            if let Some(output_tokens) = exec_tokens.output_tokens {\n1646\t                tracing::Span::current().record(\"gen_ai.usage.output_tokens\", output_tokens);\n1647\t            }\n1648\t\n1649\t            // Set agent.dispatch span status based on exit code: 0 = Ok, non-zero = Error\n1650\t            if result.exit_code != 0 {\n1651\t                tracing::Span::current().record(\"otel.status_code\", 2u64);\n1652\t                tracing::Span::current().record(\n1653\t                    \"otel.status_description\",\n1654\t                    format!(\"exit_code: {}\", result.exit_code),\n1655\t                );\n1656\t            }\n1657\t\n1658\t            was_interrupted = self.shutdown.load(Ordering::SeqCst);\n1659\t            Some(result)\n1660\t        };\n1661\t\n1662\t        let output = match exec_result {\n1663\t            Some(ref result) => AgentOutcome {\n1664\t                exit_code: result.exit_code,\n1665\t                stdout: result.stdout.clone(),\n1666\t                stderr: result.stderr.clone(),\n1667\t            },\n1668\t            None => AgentOutcome {\n1669\t                exit_code: 130, // Simulated SIGINT\n1670\t                stdout: String::new(),\n1671\t                stderr: \"interrupted before execution\".to_string(),\n1672\t            },\n1673\t        };\n1674\t\n1675\t        // Extract tokens and compute cost for effort tracking.\n1676\t        let tokens =\n1677\t            dispatch::extract_tokens(&adapter.token_extraction, &output.stdout, &output.stderr);\n1678\t        let model_name = adapter.model.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"\");\n1679\t        let estimated_cost = cost::estimate_cost(&tokens, model_name, &self.config.pricing);\n1680\t\n1681\t        if let Some(ref mut effort) = self.last_effort {\n1682\t            effort.agent_name = adapter.name.clone();\n1683\t            effort.model = adapter.model.clone();\n1684\t            effort.tokens = tokens;\n1685\t            effort.estimated_cost_usd = estimated_cost;\n1686\t        }\n1687\t\n1688\t        self.exec_output = Some((output, was_interrupted));\n1689\t        self.set_state(WorkerState::Handling)?;\n1690\t        Ok(())\n1691\t    }\n1692\t\n1693\t    /// HANDLING: classify outcome and route to handler.\n1694\t    async fn do_handle(&mut self) -> Result<()> {\n1695\t        let bead = match self.current_bead {\n1696\t            Some(ref b) => b.clone(),\n1697\t            None => {\n1698\t                bail!(\"HANDLING state without current_bead — invariant violated\");\n1699\t            }\n1700\t        };\n1701\t\n1702\t        let (output, was_interrupted) = match self.exec_output.take() {\n1703\t            Some(pair) => pair,\n1704\t            None => {\n1705\t                bail!(\"HANDLING state without exec_output — invariant violated\");\n1706\t            }\n1707\t        };\n1708\t\n1709\t        // Emit an initial heartbeat event to signal we've entered HANDLING state.\n1710\t        // This provides immediate visibility in the JSONL log when handling starts.\n1711\t        // Use emit_try_lock() to avoid blocking if telemetry writer is stuck.\n1712\t        let _ = self.telemetry.emit_try_lock(EventKind::HeartbeatEmitted {\n1713\t            bead_id: Some(bead.id.clone()),\n1714\t            state: \"HANDLING\".to_string(),\n1715\t        });\n1716\t\n1717\t        // Create a cancellation flag that can be used to abort the outcome handler\n1718\t        // if it hangs. This is a workaround for tokio::time::timeout not cancelling\n1719\t        // the future - it just stops waiting for it.\n1720\t        use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicBool, Ordering};\n1721\t        use std::sync::Arc;\n1722\t        let cancelled = Arc::new(AtomicBool::new(false));\n1723\t\n1724\t        // Spawn a background task that emits heartbeat telemetry events every 5 seconds.\n1725\t        // This allows external monitoring to detect hangs in HANDLING state without\n1726\t        // waiting for the slower heartbeat file interval (default 60s).\n1727\t        let bead_id_for_heartbeat = bead.id.clone();\n1728\t        let telemetry_for_heartbeat = self.telemetry.clone();\n1729\t        let cancelled_for_heartbeat = cancelled.clone();\n1730\t        let watchdog_for_heartbeat = self.watchdog_triggered.clone();\n1731\t        let heartbeat_task = tokio::spawn(async move {\n1732\t            let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(std::time::Duration::from_secs(5));\n1733\t            loop {\n1734\t                interval.tick().await;\n1735\t                // Check if we've been cancelled and stop emitting if so.\n1736\t                if cancelled_for_heartbeat.load(Ordering::Relaxed) {\n1737\t                    break;\n1738\t                }\n1739\t                // Check if the watchdog has triggered and force recovery if so.\n1740\t                // This allows the watchdog thread to interrupt HANDLING state even if\n1741\t                // the tokio runtime is wedged (the watchdog runs in a separate thread).\n1742\t                if watchdog_for_heartbeat.load(Ordering::Relaxed) {\n1743\t                    tracing::error!(\n1744\t                        bead_id = %bead_id_for_heartbeat,\n1745\t                        \"heartbeat task detected watchdog trigger, forcing cancellation\"\n1746\t                    );\n1747\t                    // Set the cancelled flag to abort any in-flight br calls.\n1748\t                    cancelled_for_heartbeat.store(true, Ordering::Release);\n1749\t                    break;\n1750\t                }\n1751\t                // Use emit_try_lock() to avoid blocking if telemetry writer is stuck.\n1752\t                let _ = telemetry_for_heartbeat.emit_try_lock(EventKind::HeartbeatEmitted {\n1753\t                    bead_id: Some(bead_id_for_heartbeat.clone()),\n1754\t                    state: \"HANDLING\".to_string(),\n1755\t                });\n1756\t            }\n1757\t        });\n1758\t\n1759\t        // Clone values needed for error handling before creating the async block.\n1760\t        // This avoids borrowing issues with the async block that captures `self`.\n1761\t        let bead_id_clone = bead.id.clone();\n1762\t        let store_clone = self.store.clone();\n1763\t        let telemetry_clone = self.telemetry.clone();\n1764\t        let cancelled_clone = cancelled.clone();\n1765\t\n1766\t        // Wrap the entire HANDLING state in a timeout to prevent indefinite hangs.\n1767\t        // Even if the Tokio runtime gets blocked by a synchronous operation, this\n1768\t        // timeout will fire (on a threadpool) and allow recovery.\n1769\t        let handling_future = async {\n1770\t            // Wrap the outcome handler in a 60-second timeout to prevent indefinite hangs.\n1771\t            // The health monitor's background thread writes heartbeat files based on\n1772\t            // shared state, so external monitoring can detect hangs via stale heartbeats.\n1773\t\n1774\t            // Enter the bead.outcome span for the outcome handling phase.\n1775\t            let outcome_span = tracing::info_span!(\n1776\t                \"bead.outcome\",\n1777\t                needle.bead.id = %bead.id,\n1778\t                needle.outcome = tracing::field::Empty, // Will be set based on handler result\n1779\t                needle.outcome.action = tracing::field::Empty, // Will be set based on handler result\n1780\t            );\n1781\t            let _outcome_enter = outcome_span.enter();\n1782\t\n1783\t            let Worker loop: the core NEEDLE state machine.\n//!\n//! The Worker executes the strand waterfall, claims beads, dispatches the\n//! agent, handles outcomes, and emits telemetry for every transition.\n//!\n//! State transitions are explicit — there is no implicit fallthrough and no\n//! state that does not have a defined handler. The worker emits telemetry for\n//! every transition.\n//!\n//! Depends on: `strand`, `claim`, `prompt`, `dispatch`, `outcome`,\n//!             `bead_store`, `telemetry`, `health`, `config`, `types`.\n\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\nuse std::path::PathBuf;\nuse std::sync::atomic::{AtomicBool, Ordering};\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::sync::Mutex;\nuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\n\nuse anyhow::{bail, Context, Result};\n\n#[cfg(unix)]\nuse std::sync::atomic::{AtomicU32, AtomicUsize, Ordering as AtomicOrdering};\n\nuse crate::bead_store::BeadStore;\nuse crate::canary::CanaryRunner;\nuse crate::claim::Claimer;\nuse crate::commit_hook;\nuse crate::config::{Config, ConfigLoader};\nuse crate::cost::{self, BudgetCheck, EffortData};\nuse crate::dispatch::{self, Dispatcher};\nuse crate::health::HealthMonitor;\nuse crate::mitosis::MitosisEvaluator;\nuse crate::outcome::OutcomeHandler;\nuse crate::prompt::{BuiltPrompt, PromptBuilder};\nuse crate::rate_limit::RateLimiter;\nuse crate::registry::{Registry, WorkerEntry};\nuse crate::strand::StrandRunner;\nuse crate::telemetry::{EventKind, Telemetry};\nuse crate::types::{AgentOutcome, Bead, BeadId, ClaimResult, IdleAction, Outcome, WorkerState};\nuse crate::upgrade::{self, HotReloadCheck};\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Global shutdown flag for signal handlers\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Global pointer to the shutdown flag, used by synchronous signal handlers.\n/// This is necessary because signal handlers run in a separate context and\n/// cannot easily access the Worker's shutdown flag directly.\n#[cfg(unix)]\nstatic GLOBAL_SHUTDOWN_FLAG: AtomicUsize = AtomicUsize::new(0);\n\n/// Set the global shutdown flag pointer.\n/// Called by `install_signal_handlers` to register the shutdown flag.\n#[cfg(unix)]\nfn set_global_shutdown_flag(ptr: usize) {\n    GLOBAL_SHUTDOWN_FLAG.store(ptr, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n}\n\n/// Clear the global shutdown flag pointer.\n/// Called when the worker is dropped to avoid dangling pointers.\n#[cfg(unix)]\nfn clear_global_shutdown_flag() {\n    GLOBAL_SHUTDOWN_FLAG.store(0, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n    LAST_SIGNAL.store(0, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n}\n\n/// Track the last received signal for diagnostic logging.\n/// AtomicU32 allows lock-free reads/writes from the signal handler.\n#[cfg(unix)]\nstatic LAST_SIGNAL: AtomicU32 = AtomicU32::new(0);\n\n/// Synchronous signal handler for SIGTERM, SIGINT, and SIGHUP.\n///\n/// This function is called directly by the OS when a signal is received.\n/// It must be async-signal-safe: no allocation, no locking, no I/O.\n/// We set the atomic shutdown flag, record the signal number, and return immediately.\n#[cfg(unix)]\nextern \"C\" fn signal_handler(sig: i32) {\n    // SAFETY: The signal handler is only installed after set_global_shutdown_flag\n    // has been called with a valid pointer. The pointer remains valid for the\n    // entire lifetime of the worker process.\n    let ptr = GLOBAL_SHUTDOWN_FLAG.load(AtomicOrdering::SeqCst) as *const AtomicBool;\n    if !ptr.is_null() {\n        // SAFETY: The pointer is valid and points to an AtomicBool that lives\n        // for the entire program duration.\n        unsafe {\n            (*ptr).store(true, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n        }\n        // Record the signal number so the main loop can log it.\n        LAST_SIGNAL.store(sig as u32, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n    }\n}\n\n/// Install synchronous signal handlers for SIGTERM, SIGINT, and SIGHUP.\n///\n/// Uses libc::sigaction to register handlers that set the shutdown flag\n/// immediately when a signal is received. This ensures that signals are\n/// caught even if the tokio runtime hasn't polled async signal tasks yet.\n#[cfg(unix)]\nunsafe fn install_unix_signal_handlers() {\n    use libc::{sigaction, sigemptyset, SA_RESTART, SIGHUP, SIGINT, SIGTERM};\n\n    // Set up the sigaction structure.\n    let mut act: libc::sigaction = std::mem::zeroed();\n    act.sa_sigaction = signal_handler as *const () as usize;\n    // Block all signals during handler execution to prevent re-entrancy issues.\n    sigemptyset(&mut act.sa_mask as *mut libc::sigset_t);\n    // Use SA_RESTART to automatically restart system calls interrupted by signals.\n    act.sa_flags = SA_RESTART;\n\n    // Install handlers for SIGTERM, SIGINT, and SIGHUP.\n    // We ignore errors here - if a handler can't be installed, we'll log a\n    // warning but continue. The async handlers (below) provide a fallback.\n    for &sig in &[SIGTERM, SIGINT, SIGHUP] {\n        let mut old: libc::sigaction = std::mem::zeroed();\n        if sigaction(sig, &act, &mut old) == 0 {\n            tracing::debug!(signal = sig, \"installed synchronous signal handler\");\n        } else {\n            // Log the error but don't fail - the async handlers provide a fallback.\n            tracing::warn!(\n                signal = sig,\n                errno = *libc::__errno_location(),\n                \"failed to install synchronous signal handler\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Global state for atexit handler to emit worker.stopped on unexpected termination.\n///\n/// When a worker is killed externally (e.g., SIGKILL from capacity governor),\n/// the normal signal handlers don't run. The atexit handler provides a last-resort\n/// mechanism to emit worker.stopped telemetry with diagnostic information.\nstatic ATEXIT_WORKER_STATE: Mutex<Option<AtexitWorkerState>> = Mutex::new(None);\n\n/// State captured for the atexit handler.\nstruct AtexitWorkerState {\n    worker_name: String,\n    beads_processed: u64,\n    start_time: Instant,\n    last_state: String,\n    log_file_path: Option<String>,\n    /// Path to this worker's heartbeat file for cleanup on unexpected termination.\n    heartbeat_path: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Register the atexit handler with worker state.\n///\n/// Called by `install_signal_handlers` to ensure the atexit handler can\n/// emit meaningful telemetry if the process terminates unexpectedly.\nfn register_atexit_handler(\n    worker_name: String,\n    beads_processed: u64,\n    start_time: Instant,\n    last_state: String,\n    log_file_path: Option<String>,\n    heartbeat_path: Option<String>,\n) {\n    let state = AtexitWorkerState {\n        worker_name,\n        beads_processed,\n        start_time,\n        last_state,\n        log_file_path,\n        heartbeat_path,\n    };\n    *ATEXIT_WORKER_STATE.lock().unwrap() = Some(state);\n\n    // Register the atexit handler.\n    // This will run when the process exits normally, but NOT on SIGKILL.\n    extern \"C\" fn atexit_handler() {\n        if let Some(state) = ATEXIT_WORKER_STATE.lock().unwrap().as_ref() {\n            let uptime = state.start_time.elapsed().as_secs();\n            // Try to write to stderr as a last resort since telemetry may be unavailable.\n            eprintln!(\n                \"NEEDLE worker '{}' stopped unexpectedly: state={}, beads_processed={}, uptime={}s\",\n                state.worker_name, state.last_state, state.beads_processed, uptime\n            );\n            eprintln!(\"This indicates the worker was killed by an external process (e.g., SIGKILL, OOM, capacity governor)\");\n\n            // Try to clean up the heartbeat file to prevent stale detection.\n            // Use the proper cleanup function with error handling from the health module.\n            if let Some(ref hb_path) = state.heartbeat_path {\n                use std::path::Path;\n                // Import the cleanup function from health module\n                // This function has proper error handling: logs, doesn't panic,\n                // and returns Ok(()) even if removal fails (best-effort cleanup).\n                if let Err(e) = crate::health::cleanup_heartbeat_file(Path::new(hb_path)) {\n                    eprintln!(\"Heartbeat cleanup error: {}\", e);\n                } else {\n                    eprintln!(\"Cleaned up heartbeat file: {}\", hb_path);\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Try to write a worker.stopped event to the JSONL log file.\n            // This provides diagnostic information even when the worker is killed abruptly.\n            if let Some(ref log_path) = state.log_file_path {\n                use std::fs::OpenOptions;\n                use std::io::Write;\n\n                let event = serde_json::json!({\n                    \"event_type\": \"worker.stopped\",\n                    \"timestamp\": chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339_opts(chrono::SecondsFormat::Secs, true),\n                    \"data\": {\n                        \"worker_id\": state.worker_name,\n                        \"reason\": \"external_kill\",\n                        \"beads_processed\": state.beads_processed,\n                        \"uptime_secs\": uptime,\n                        \"final_state\": state.last_state,\n                        \"via_atexit_handler\": true\n                    }\n                });\n\n                if let Ok(mut file) = OpenOptions::new().create(true).append(true).open(log_path) {\n                    let _ = writeln!(file, \"{}\", event);\n                    let _ = file.flush();\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // SAFETY: atexit is safe to call with a function pointer.\n    unsafe {\n        libc::atexit(atexit_handler);\n    }\n}\n\n/// Update the atexit state when the worker state changes.\n///\n/// Called by `set_state` to keep the atexit handler's last state fresh.\nfn update_atexit_state(last_state: String) {\n    if let Some(state) = ATEXIT_WORKER_STATE.lock().unwrap().as_mut() {\n        state.last_state = last_state;\n    }\n}\n\n/// Stub implementations for non-Unix platforms.\n/// These functions are no-ops on platforms where Unix signals are not available.\n#[cfg(not(unix))]\nfn set_global_shutdown_flag(_ptr: usize) {\n    // No-op on non-Unix platforms\n}\n\n#[cfg(not(unix))]\nfn clear_global_shutdown_flag() {\n    // No-op on non-Unix platforms\n}\n\n#[cfg(not(unix))]\nfn register_atexit_handler(\n    _worker_name: String,\n    _beads_processed: u64,\n    _start_time: Instant,\n    _last_state: String,\n    _log_file_path: Option<String>,\n    _heartbeat_path: Option<String>,\n) {\n    // No-op on non-Unix platforms\n}\n\n#[cfg(not(unix))]\nfn update_atexit_state(_last_state: String) {\n    // No-op on non-Unix platforms\n}\n\n/// TTL for race-lost bead exclusions.\n///\n/// After losing a claim race, a bead is excluded from selection for this duration\n/// to prevent infinite loops where the selector returns the same bead repeatedly.\nconst RACE_LOST_EXCLUSION_TTL: Duration = Duration::from_secs(30);\n\n/// Timeout for HANDLING state watchdog.\n///\n/// If the worker remains in HANDLING state for longer than this duration,\n/// the watchdog thread will force a recovery. This is longer than the\n/// inner timeouts (50s, 60s, 90s) to allow normal recovery to work first.\nconst HANDLING_WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT_SECS: u64 = 120;\n\n/// The NEEDLE worker — owns and drives the full state machine.\npub struct Worker {\n    config: Config,\n    worker_name: String,\n    store: Arc<dyn BeadStore>,\n    /// Home workspace store — kept for restore after processing a remote bead.\n    home_store: Arc<dyn BeadStore>,\n    telemetry: Telemetry,\n    strands: StrandRunner,\n    claimer: Claimer,\n    prompt_builder: PromptBuilder,\n    dispatcher: Dispatcher,\n    outcome_handler: OutcomeHandler,\n    health: HealthMonitor,\n    registry: Registry,\n    rate_limiter: RateLimiter,\n    mitosis_evaluator: MitosisEvaluator,\n\n    // State machine fields\n    state: WorkerState,\n    current_bead: Option<Bead>,\n    exclusion_set: HashSet<BeadId>,\n    /// Race-lost exclusions with TTL — prevents re-selecting beads that just lost a claim race.\n    /// Each entry is (bead_id, expiration_time). Entries are pruned on access.\n    race_lost_exclusions: Vec<(BeadId, Instant)>,\n    /// Beads that lost a claim race in the current selection cycle.\n    /// These are added to exclusion_set to prevent immediate re-selection.\n    /// Cleared at the start of the next SELECTING cycle.\n    race_lost_this_cycle: HashSet<BeadId>,\n    retry_count: u32,\n    consecutive_race_lost: u32,\n    beads_processed: u64,\n    shutdown: Arc<AtomicBool>,\n    last_error: Option<anyhow::Error>,\n    boot_time: Option<Instant>,\n\n    // Transient fields — pass data between state handlers within a single cycle.\n    built_prompt: Option<BuiltPrompt>,\n    current_strand: Option<String>,\n    exec_output: Option<(AgentOutcome, bool)>,\n    /// Effort tracking data for the current bead cycle.\n    last_effort: Option<EffortData>,\n    /// HEAD SHA captured just before agent dispatch; used to detect new commits.\n    pre_dispatch_head: Option<String>,\n    /// The workspace of the current bead store — updated when switching to remote.\n    /// Used to ensure heartbeat reports the actual workspace where work is happening.\n    current_workspace: PathBuf,\n    /// Whether `worker.booting` was already emitted externally (e.g., from CLI layer).\n    /// When true, `run()` skips emitting the booting event to avoid duplicates.\n    booting_emitted: bool,\n    /// Waterfall restart count from the most recent select cycle (for exhausted telemetry).\n    last_waterfall_restarts: u32,\n    /// Names of strands that triggered waterfall restarts in the most recent cycle.\n    last_restart_triggers: Vec<String>,\n    /// Strand evaluations from the most recent select cycle (for exhausted telemetry).\n    last_strand_evaluations: Vec<(String, String, u64)>,\n    /// Timestamp when the worker entered HANDLING state.\n    /// Used by the watchdog to detect stuck HANDLING state.\n    handling_state_entered_at: Option<Instant>,\n    /// Flag set by the watchdog thread when HANDLING state timeout is detected.\n    /// The main worker loop checks this flag and forces recovery if set.\n    watchdog_triggered: Arc<AtomicBool>,\n    /// Handle to the watchdog thread for cleanup on worker drop.\n    #[allow(dead_code)]\n    watchdog_handle: Option<std::thread::JoinHandle<()>>,\n    /// The current bead lifecycle span guard. Created when a bead is claimed,\n    /// dropped when the bead lifecycle ends (after HANDLING or when the bead is released).\n    #[allow(dead_code)]\n    bead_lifecycle_span: Option<tracing::span::EnteredSpan>,\n    /// The last outcome for the current bead (used to record on bead.lifecycle span).\n    last_outcome: Option<String>,\n}\n\nimpl Worker {\n    /// Construct a worker using a pre-existing telemetry instance.\n    ///\n    /// Use this when telemetry has already been started (e.g. after emitting\n    /// `worker.booting` from the CLI layer) so that early init steps are\n    /// visible in the JSONL log.\n    pub fn new_with_telemetry(\n        config: Config,\n        worker_name: String,\n        store: Arc<dyn BeadStore>,\n        telemetry: Telemetry,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self::build(config, worker_name, store, telemetry, true)\n    }\n\n    /// Construct a worker from config, a worker name, and a bead store implementation.\n    ///\n    /// Creates its own telemetry instance. Prefer [`new_with_telemetry`] when\n    /// the caller has already created and started telemetry for early boot\n    /// diagnostics.\n    pub fn new(config: Config, worker_name: String, store: Arc<dyn BeadStore>) -> Self {\n        let qualified_id = format!(\"{}-{}\", config.agent.default, worker_name);\n        let telemetry = Telemetry::from_config(qualified_id.clone(), &config.telemetry)\n            .unwrap_or_else(|e| {\n                tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"failed to create hook-enabled telemetry, falling back\");\n                Telemetry::new(qualified_id.clone())\n            });\n        Self::build(config, worker_name, store, telemetry, false)\n    }\n\n    /// Shared construction logic used by both [`new`] and [`new_with_telemetry`].\n    fn build(\n        config: Config,\n        worker_name: String,\n        store: Arc<dyn BeadStore>,\n        telemetry: Telemetry,\n        booting_emitted: bool,\n    ) -> Self {\n        let qualified_id = format!(\"{}-{}\", config.agent.default, worker_name);\n        let strand_registry = Registry::default_location(&config.workspace.home);\n        let strands =\n            StrandRunner::from_config(&config, &qualified_id, strand_registry, telemetry.clone());\n        let claimer = Claimer::new(\n            store.clone(),\n            std::path::PathBuf::from(\"/tmp\"),\n            config.worker.max_claim_retries,\n            100,\n            telemetry.clone(),\n        );\n        let prompt_builder = PromptBuilder::with_workspace(\n            &config.prompt,\n            &config.workspace.default,\n        )\n        .unwrap_or_else(|e| {\n            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"failed to load workspace learnings, using default prompt builder\");\n            PromptBuilder::new(&config.prompt)\n        })\n        .with_cross_workspace_skills(\n            &config.strands.explore.workspaces,\n            &config.workspace.labels,\n        )\n        .with_global_learnings(&config.strands.learning.global_learnings_file);\n        let dispatcher = match Dispatcher::new(&config, telemetry.clone()) {\n            Ok(d) => d,\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"failed to load adapters, using built-in defaults\");\n                let builtins = crate::dispatch::builtin_adapters()\n                    .into_iter()\n                    .map(|a| (a.name.clone(), a))\n                    .collect();\n                Dispatcher::with_adapters(builtins, telemetry.clone(), config.agent.timeout)\n            }\n        };\n        let outcome_handler = OutcomeHandler::new(config.clone(), telemetry.clone());\n\n        // Create the shutdown flag BEFORE creating HealthMonitor so we can share it.\n        // This ensures that when the heartbeat emitter's circuit breaker fires,\n        // it sets the worker's shutdown flag (not its own private flag), allowing\n        // the main worker loop to gracefully stop with worker.stopped telemetry.\n        let shutdown = Arc::new(AtomicBool::new(false));\n\n        let health = HealthMonitor::new(\n            config.clone(),\n            worker_name.clone(),\n            telemetry.clone(),\n            Some(shutdown.clone()),\n        );\n        let registry = Registry::default_location(&config.workspace.home);\n        let rate_limiter =\n            RateLimiter::new(config.limits.clone(), &config.workspace.home.join(\"state\"));\n        let mitosis_evaluator = MitosisEvaluator::new(\n            config.strands.mitosis.clone(),\n            telemetry.clone(),\n            std::path::PathBuf::from(\"/tmp\"),\n        );\n\n        // Restore beads_processed from registry if this worker was previously registered\n        // (e.g., hot-reload resume). New workers start at 0.\n        // Match by qualified identity ({adapter}-{worker_id}).\n        let qualified_id = format!(\"{}-{}\", config.agent.default, worker_name);\n        let beads_processed = registry\n            .list()\n            .ok()\n            .and_then(|workers| workers.into_iter().find(|w| w.id == qualified_id))\n            .map(|entry| entry.beads_processed)\n            .unwrap_or(0);\n\n        let default_workspace = config.workspace.default.clone();\n\n        // Create the watchdog trigger flag before creating the Worker.\n        let watchdog_triggered = Arc::new(AtomicBool::new(false));\n\n        Worker {\n            config,\n            worker_name,\n            home_store: store.clone(),\n            store,\n            telemetry,\n            strands,\n            claimer,\n            prompt_builder,\n            dispatcher,\n            outcome_handler,\n            health,\n            registry,\n            rate_limiter,\n            mitosis_evaluator,\n            state: WorkerState::Booting,\n            current_bead: None,\n            exclusion_set: HashSet::new(),\n            race_lost_exclusions: Vec::new(),\n            race_lost_this_cycle: HashSet::new(),\n            retry_count: 0,\n            consecutive_race_lost: 0,\n            beads_processed,\n            shutdown,\n            last_error: None,\n            boot_time: None,\n            built_prompt: None,\n            current_strand: None,\n            exec_output: None,\n            last_effort: None,\n            pre_dispatch_head: None,\n            current_workspace: default_workspace,\n            booting_emitted,\n            last_waterfall_restarts: 0,\n            last_restart_triggers: Vec::new(),\n            last_strand_evaluations: Vec::new(),\n            handling_state_entered_at: None,\n            watchdog_triggered: watchdog_triggered.clone(),\n            watchdog_handle: None,\n            bead_lifecycle_span: None,\n            last_outcome: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Start the watchdog thread that monitors HANDLING state duration.\n    ///\n    /// The watchdog runs in a separate thread (not part of the Tokio runtime)\n    /// and can detect when the worker is stuck in HANDLING state even if\n    /// the Tokio runtime becomes wedged. If HANDLING state exceeds the\n    /// timeout, the watchdog sets the `watchdog_triggered` flag, which\n    /// the main worker loop checks to force recovery.\n    fn start_watchdog_thread(&mut self) {\n        let watchdog_triggered = self.watchdog_triggered.clone();\n        let handling_state_entered_at_ptr =\n            &self.handling_state_entered_at as *const Option<Instant> as usize;\n\n        let handle = std::thread::spawn(move || {\n            loop {\n                std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_secs(5));\n                if watchdog_triggered.load(Ordering::Relaxed) {\n                    // Watchdog has already triggered, exit the thread.\n                    break;\n                }\n                // Check if we've been in HANDLING state for too long.\n                // We read the timestamp from the Worker struct via the pointer.\n                // SAFETY: The Worker struct outlives the watchdog thread because\n                // the thread is joined when the Worker is dropped.\n                let entered_at = unsafe {\n                    let ptr = handling_state_entered_at_ptr as *const Option<Instant>;\n                    (*ptr).as_ref().copied()\n                };\n\n                if let Some(entry_time) = entered_at {\n                    let elapsed = entry_time.elapsed().as_secs();\n                    if elapsed >= HANDLING_WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT_SECS {\n                        tracing::error!(\n                            elapsed_secs = elapsed,\n                            \"HANDLING state watchdog triggered - forcing recovery\"\n                        );\n                        watchdog_triggered.store(true, Ordering::Release);\n                        break;\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        });\n\n        self.watchdog_handle = Some(handle);\n    }\n\n    /// Run the worker loop until exhausted, stopped, or errored.\n    ///\n    /// The main loop is a match on `self.state`. Every state has a handler\n    /// that performs its actions and sets `self.state` to the next state.\n    ///\n    /// Guarantees that the telemetry BufWriter is flushed before returning,\n    /// even when the inner state machine exits early via `?`.\n    pub async fn run(&mut self) -> Result<WorkerState> {\n        // Start the telemetry writer now that we are inside the tokio runtime.\n        self.telemetry.start();\n\n        // IMMEDIATE boot event — must be the first thing emitted after telemetry starts.\n        // This ensures we get a trace even if subsequent init steps block indefinitely.\n        // Skip if already emitted externally (e.g., from CLI layer for early boot diagnostics).\n        if !self.booting_emitted {\n            self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::WorkerBooting {\n                worker_name: self.worker_name.clone(),\n                version: env!(\"CARGO_PKG_VERSION\").to_string(),\n            })?;\n            // Force-flush to disk before boot() — if init blocks, we still have a trace.\n            self.telemetry\n                .force_flush_async(std::time::Duration::from_secs(5))\n                .await?;\n        }\n\n        let result = self.run_inner().await;\n\n        // Safety-net flush: shutdown() is idempotent. Normal terminal paths\n        // (stop, handle_exhausted, Errored) already call it; this catches\n        // any early-exit via `?` (boot failure, state handler panic, etc.)\n        // so the BufWriter is always flushed before the tokio Runtime drops.\n        self.telemetry.shutdown().await;\n\n        result\n    }\n\n    /// Inner state machine — called only from [`run()`](Self::run).\n    ///\n    /// May return early via `?` without calling `telemetry.shutdown()`;\n    /// `run()` handles the safety-net flush.\n    async fn run_inner(&mut self) -> Result<WorkerState> {\n        // Boot: validate config and initialize.\n        self.boot()?;\n\n        // Start the watchdog thread that monitors HANDLING state duration.\n        // This must be started after boot() so the Worker struct is fully initialized.\n        self.start_watchdog_thread();\n\n        // Install signal handlers.\n        self.install_signal_handlers();\n\n        // Create the worker.session root span that encompasses the entire worker lifecycle.\n        let worker_id = self.qualified_id();\n        let session_span = tracing::info_span!(\n            \"worker.session\",\n            needle.worker_id = %worker_id,\n            needle.session_id = %self.telemetry.session_id(),\n            needle.agent = %self.config.agent.default,\n            needle.model = %self.config.agent.default, // Will be updated when adapter is resolved\n            needle.workspace = %self.config.workspace.default.display(),\n        );\n        let _enter = session_span.enter();\n\n        loop {\n            // Check for shutdown signal between states.\n            if self.shutdown.load(Ordering::SeqCst) {\n                // Retrieve and clear the last received signal for logging.\n                #[cfg(unix)]\n                let signal_name = {\n                    let sig = LAST_SIGNAL.swap(0, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n                    if sig == 0 {\n                        None\n                    } else {\n                        Some(match sig {\n                            1 => \"SIGHUP\",\n                            2 => \"SIGINT\",\n                            15 => \"SIGTERM\",\n                            _ => \"unknown signal\",\n                        })\n                    }\n                };\n                #[cfg(not(unix))]\n                let signal_name = None;\n\n                let reason = if let Some(name) = signal_name {\n                    format!(\"signal received ({name})\")\n                } else {\n                    \"signal received\".to_string()\n                };\n\n                match self.state {\n                    // If we're in the middle of processing a bead, release it\n                    // before stopping. This ensures the bead is returned to\n                    // the open state and can be claimed by another worker.\n                    WorkerState::Building\n                    | WorkerState::Dispatching\n                    | WorkerState::Executing\n                    | WorkerState::Handling => {\n                        // Release any claimed bead before stopping.\n                        self.release_current_bead(&reason).await;\n                        return self.stop(&reason).await;\n                    }\n                    // For states where we don't hold a bead, stop immediately.\n                    WorkerState::Selecting\n                    | WorkerState::Claiming\n                    | WorkerState::Retrying\n                    | WorkerState::Logging => {\n                        // Release any claimed bead before stopping (should be none in these states).\n                        self.release_current_bead(&reason).await;\n                        return self.stop(&reason).await;\n                    }\n                    WorkerState::Stopped | WorkerState::Exhausted | WorkerState::Errored => {\n                        return self.stop(&reason).await;\n                    }\n                    WorkerState::Booting => {\n                        return self.stop(\"signal received during boot\").await;\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Check for watchdog trigger - this indicates HANDLING state is wedged.\n            // The watchdog runs in a separate thread and can detect when the worker\n            // is stuck even if the Tokio runtime becomes unresponsive.\n            if self.watchdog_triggered.load(Ordering::Relaxed)\n                && self.state == WorkerState::Handling\n            {\n                tracing::error!(\"watchdog detected HANDLING state hang, forcing recovery\");\n                // Emit critical timeout event.\n                let bead_id = self.current_bead.as_ref().map(|b| b.id.clone());\n                let _ = self\n                    .telemetry\n                    .emit_try_lock(EventKind::WorkerHandlingTimeout {\n                        bead_id: bead_id.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| BeadId::from(\"unknown\")),\n                        outcome: \"unknown\".to_string(),\n                        operation: \"watchdog\".to_string(),\n                        error: format!(\n                            \"HANDLING state exceeded {}s timeout\",\n                            HANDLING_WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT_SECS\n                        ),\n                    });\n                // Attempt best-effort release if we have a bead.\n                if let Some(ref bead) = self.current_bead {\n                    let bead_id = bead.id.clone();\n                    tracing::warn!(bead_id = %bead_id, \"best-effort bead release due to watchdog timeout\");\n                    let _ =\n                        tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(30), self.store.release(&bead_id))\n                            .await;\n                }\n                // Clear the watchdog trigger and force transition to LOGGING.\n                self.watchdog_triggered.store(false, Ordering::Release);\n                self.handling_state_entered_at = None;\n                // Force transition to LOGGING to recover.\n                self.set_state(WorkerState::Logging)?;\n            }\n\n            match self.state {\n                WorkerState::Selecting => self.do_select().await?,\n                WorkerState::Claiming => self.do_claim().await?,\n                WorkerState::Retrying => self.do_retry().await?,\n                WorkerState::Building => self.do_build().await?,\n                WorkerState::Dispatching => self.do_dispatch().await?,\n                WorkerState::Executing => self.do_execute().await?,\n                WorkerState::Handling => self.do_handle().await?,\n                WorkerState::Logging => self.do_log()?,\n                WorkerState::Exhausted => {\n                    let next = self.handle_exhausted().await?;\n                    match next {\n                        WorkerState::Selecting => continue,\n                        terminal => return Ok(terminal),\n                    }\n                }\n                WorkerState::Stopped => {\n                    return self.stop(\"normal shutdown\").await;\n                }\n                WorkerState::Errored => {\n                    let err = self\n                        .last_error\n                        .take()\n                        .unwrap_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!(\"unknown error\"));\n                    let msg = format!(\"{err}\");\n                    self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::WorkerErrored {\n                        error_type: \"worker_scoped\".to_string(),\n                        error_message: msg.clone(),\n                        beads_processed: self.beads_processed,\n                    })?;\n\n                    // Emit WorkerStopped before exiting so telemetry shows a clean shutdown.\n                    // This ensures operators can distinguish \"exited with error\" from\n                    // \"killed by external agent\" (e.g., SIGKILL, OOM).\n                    let uptime = self.boot_time.map(|t| t.elapsed().as_secs()).unwrap_or(0);\n                    let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::WorkerStopped {\n                        reason: format!(\"error: {msg}\"),\n                        beads_processed: self.beads_processed,\n                        uptime_secs: uptime,\n                    });\n\n                    // Best-effort stop heartbeat on error.\n                    self.health.stop();\n                    // Best-effort deregister on error.\n                    if let Err(e) = self.registry.deregister(&self.qualified_id()) {\n                        tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"failed to deregister from worker registry on error\");\n                    }\n                    self.telemetry.shutdown().await;\n                    return Err(err);\n                }\n                WorkerState::Booting => {\n                    bail!(\"boot() should have transitioned past Booting state\");\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ── Boot ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    /// Validate configuration and initialize the worker.\n    ///\n    /// Each step is instrumented with `init.step.started`/`init.step.completed`\n    /// events so that hangs are visible in the telemetry log. Boot duration is\n    /// capped at 60 seconds — if exceeded, the worker self-aborts with a\n    /// `worker.boot.timeout` event and exits with a non-zero code.\n    fn boot(&mut self) -> Result<()> {\n        self.boot_time = Some(Instant::now());\n        const BOOT_TIMEOUT_SECS: u64 = 60;\n\n        // Step: Config validation\n        self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::InitStepStarted {\n            step: \"config_validation\".to_string(),\n        })?;\n        let step_start = Instant::now();\n        let errors = ConfigLoader::validate(&self.config);\n        if !errors.is_empty() {\n            let msg = errors\n                .iter()\n                .map(|e| e.to_string())\n                .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n                .join(\"; \");\n            bail!(\"config validation failed: {msg}\");\n        }\n        self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::InitStepCompleted {\n            step: \"config_validation\".to_string(),\n            duration_ms: step_start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n        })?;\n\n        // Check boot timeout before each step\n        self.check_boot_timeout(BOOT_TIMEOUT_SECS)?;\n\n        // Step: Registry registration\n        self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::InitStepStarted {\n            step: \"registry_registration\".to_string(),\n        })?;\n        let step_start = Instant::now();\n        let qualified_id = format!(\"{}-{}\", self.config.agent.default, self.worker_name);\n        let entry = WorkerEntry {\n            id: qualified_id.clone(),\n            pid: std::process::id(),\n            workspace: self.config.workspace.default.clone(),\n            agent: self.config.agent.default.clone(),\n            model: None,\n            provider: self.resolve_provider(),\n            started_at: chrono::Utc::now(),\n            beads_processed: 0,\n        };\n        if let Err(e) = self.registry.register(entry) {\n            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"failed to register in worker registry\");\n        }\n        self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::InitStepCompleted {\n            step: \"registry_registration\".to_string(),\n            duration_ms: step_start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n        })?;\n\n        // Check boot timeout before each step\n        self.check_boot_timeout(BOOT_TIMEOUT_SECS)?;\n\n        // Step: Idle action validation\n        self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::InitStepStarted {\n            step: \"idle_action_validation\".to_string(),\n        })?;\n        let step_start = Instant::now();\n\n        // Warn if idle_action=exit without supervisor supervision\n        if self.config.worker.idle_action == crate::types::IdleAction::Exit {\n            match self.health.detect_supervisor() {\n                Ok(supervisor_present) => {\n                    if !supervisor_present {\n                        tracing::warn!(\n                            idle_action = \"exit\",\n                            \"no supervisor detected: worker configured to exit when queue is dry will leave orphaned in_progress beads with no reclaim mechanism\"\n                        );\n                        tracing::warn!(\n                            \"to fix: either run workers under a supervisor (needle supervise) or set idle_action=wait in config\"\n                        );\n                    } else {\n                        tracing::info!(\n                            idle_action = \"exit\",\n                            \"supervisor detected: exit policy is safe\"\n                        );\n                    }\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    tracing::debug!(\n                        error = %e,\n                        \"failed to detect supervisor presence, skipping idle_action validation\"\n                    );\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::InitStepCompleted {\n            step: \"idle_action_validation\".to_string(),\n            duration_ms: step_start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n        })?;\n\n        // Check boot timeout before each step\n        self.check_boot_timeout(BOOT_TIMEOUT_SECS)?;\n\n        // Step: Heartbeat emitter start\n        self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::InitStepStarted {\n            step: \"heartbeat_emitter\".to_string(),\n        })?;\n        let step_start = Instant::now();\n        if let Err(e) = self.health.start_emitter() {\n            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"failed to start heartbeat emitter\");\n        }\n        self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::InitStepCompleted {\n            step: \"heartbeat_emitter\".to_string(),\n            duration_ms: step_start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n        })?;\n\n        // Emit worker started event — boot complete\n        self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::WorkerStarted {\n            worker_name: self.worker_name.clone(),\n            version: env!(\"CARGO_PKG_VERSION\").to_string(),\n        })?;\n\n        self.set_state(WorkerState::Selecting)?;\n\n        tracing::info!(\n            worker = %self.worker_name,\n            strands = ?self.strands.strand_names(),\n            \"worker booted\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Check if boot has exceeded the timeout and abort if so.\n    ///\n    /// Emits `worker.boot.timeout` and exits the process with a non-zero\n    /// code. This is a last-resort measure when an init step hangs.\n    fn check_boot_timeout(&self, timeout_secs: u64) -> Result<()> {\n        if let Some(boot_start) = self.boot_time {\n            let elapsed = boot_start.elapsed();\n            if elapsed.as_secs() > timeout_secs {\n                let elapsed_ms = elapsed.as_millis() as u64;\n                // Emit the timeout event before aborting\n                let _ = self\n                    .telemetry\n                    .emit(EventKind::WorkerBootTimeout { elapsed_ms });\n                tracing::error!(\n                    elapsed_ms,\n                    \"boot timeout exceeded {}s — aborting\",\n                    timeout_secs\n                );\n                // Flush telemetry before exit\n                std::mem::forget(self.telemetry.clone());\n                // Exit with a distinct code to indicate boot timeout\n                std::process::exit(71); // EX_OSERR + custom offset\n            }\n        }\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    // ── Signal handling ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    /// Install SIGINT, SIGTERM, and SIGHUP handlers that set the shutdown flag.\n    ///\n    /// SIGHUP is handled because when the parent bash dies (e.g., tmux session\n    /// killed, external reaper), the child process receives SIGHUP by default.\n    /// Without a handler, the process terminates immediately without emitting\n    /// worker.stopped telemetry or flushing the telemetry buffer.\n    ///\n    /// Uses synchronous signal handlers via libc/signal-hook to ensure signals\n    /// are caught immediately, even if the tokio runtime hasn't polled async\n    /// signal tasks yet. This prevents silent process termination when signals\n    /// arrive early (e.g., SIGHUP from parent bash death during worker startup).\n    fn install_signal_handlers(&self) {\n        // Store a global reference to the shutdown flag for signal handlers.\n        // We use a leak to ensure the reference lives for the entire program duration.\n        let shutdown_ptr = Arc::into_raw(self.shutdown.clone()) as usize;\n        set_global_shutdown_flag(shutdown_ptr);\n\n        // Register atexit handler to emit worker.stopped telemetry on unexpected termination.\n        // This provides diagnostic information when the worker is killed by an external\n        // process (e.g., capacity governor, OOM killer, SIGKILL).\n        let start_time = self.boot_time.unwrap_or_else(Instant::now);\n        let heartbeat_path = self.health.heartbeat_path().to_str().map(String::from);\n        register_atexit_handler(\n            self.worker_name.clone(),\n            self.beads_processed,\n            start_time,\n            format!(\"{:?}\", self.state),\n            None, // log_file_path -
