{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_983b5313fe5b435d8c61f41e","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t![NEEDLE](docs/hero.png)\n2\t\n3\t# 🧵 NEEDLE\n4\t\n5\t[![CI](https://github.com/jedarden/NEEDLE/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/jedarden/NEEDLE/actions/workflows/ci.yml)\n6\t[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg)](LICENSE)\n7\t[![Rust](https://img.shields.io/badge/rust-1.75+-orange.svg)](rust-toolchain.toml)\n8\t[![Version](https://img.shields.io/badge/version-0.2.8-green.svg)](Cargo.toml)\n9\t\n10\t**N**avigates **E**very **E**nqueued **D**eliverable, **L**ogs **E**ffort\n11\t\n12\t> Deterministic bead processing with explicit outcome paths.\n13\t\n14\tNEEDLE is a universal wrapper for headless coding CLI agents. It processes a shared bead queue in deterministic order, dispatching work to any headless CLI (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex, Aider) and handling every outcome through an explicit, predefined path.\n15\t\n16\t---\n17\t\n18\t## 🚀 Quickstart\n19\t\n20\t```bash\n21\t# Install the latest release\n22\tcurl -fsSL https://github.com/jedarden/NEEDLE/releases/latest/download/install.sh | bash\n23\t\n24\t# Or build from source\n25\tcargo install --git https://github.com/jedarden/NEEDLE\n26\t\n27\t# Run a worker against a bead-tracked workspace\n28\tcd /path/to/your/workspace\n29\tneedle run --agent claude --identity alpha\n30\t```\n31\t\n32\tA worker starts, claims the next bead, dispatches to your chosen agent CLI, and loops. Multiple workers can run in parallel against the same workspace — coordination is handled by the shared bead queue (no central orchestrator).\n33\t\n34\tSee [`docs/examples/`](docs/examples/) for end-to-end configurations.\n35\t\n36\t---\n37\t\n38\t## 🧶 What is a bead?\n39\t\n40\tA **bead** is a work item — the unit of work NEEDLE processes. Think of it as a structured task ticket: a title, a body describing the deliverable and acceptance criteria, a status (`open`, `in_progress`, `done`), and optional metadata like priority and dependencies.\n41\t\n42\tBeads live in a **bead store** — a SQLite database (`beads.db`) plus a JSONL checkpoint file (`issues.jsonl`) in a `.beads/` directory inside your project. The `br` CLI manages the bead store: create beads, list them, claim them, close them.\n43\t\n44\t```bash\n45\t# Create a bead\n46\tbr create --title \"Add pagination to search results\" --body \"...\"\n47\t\n48\t# List open beads\n49\tbr list --status open\n50\t\n51\t# NEEDLE does the rest: claims, dispatches, and closes beads automatically\n52\t```\n53\t\n54\tNEEDLE workers read from this store, claim the next available bead atomically, dispatch it to your chosen agent CLI, and close it on success — then loop.\n55\t\n56\t---\n57\t\n58\t## 🤔 Why NEEDLE\n59\t\n60\tExisting agent orchestration tools are built for one of two shapes:\n61\t\n62\t- **Conversational frameworks** (LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI) assume a chat loop with a human-in-the-loop or another LLM. They are bad at headless, long-running, cost-bounded work.\n63\t- **Workflow engines** (Temporal, Argo Workflows, Inngest) assume each step is deterministic code. They are bad at non-deterministic agent steps whose outcomes have to be classified and routed.\n64\t\n65\tNEEDLE is the missing middle: a **deterministic state machine that drives non-deterministic agents.** Every outcome an agent can produce has an explicit handler. The agent's work is fuzzy; the orchestration around it is not.\n66\t\n67\t---\n68\t\n69\t## 🧠 Core Principle\n70\t\n71\tNEEDLE is a **state machine**, not a script. Every bead transitions through a finite set of states, and every transition has a defined handler. There are no implicit fallbacks, no swallowed errors, no undefined paths.\n72\t\n73\t```\n74\tIf an outcome can happen, it has a handler.\n75\tIf it doesn't have a handler, it cannot happen.\n76\t```\n77\t\n78\t---\n79\t\n80\t## 🔄 The NEEDLE Algorithm\n81\t\n82\tA single worker executes this loop indefinitely:\n83\t\n84\t```\n85\t┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n86\t│                                                     │\n87\t│   ┌───────────┐                                     │\n88\t│   │  🔍 SELECT │◄────────────────────────────────┐  │\n89\t│   └─────┬─────┘                                  │  │\n90\t│         │                                        │  │\n91\t│         ▼                                        │  │\n92\t│   ┌───────────┐   race lost    ┌──────────┐     │  │\n93\t│   │  🔒 CLAIM  │──────────────►│ 🔁 RETRY  │─────┘  │\n94\t│   └─────┬─────┘               └──────────┘        │\n95\t│         │ claimed                                   │\n96\t│         ▼                                           │\n97\t│   ┌───────────┐                                     │\n98\t│   │ 📋 BUILD   │                                     │\n99\t│   └─────┬─────┘                                     │\n100\t│         │                                           │\n101\t│         ▼                                           │\n102\t│   ┌───────────┐                                     │\n103\t│   │ 🚀 DISPATCH│                                     │\n104\t│   └─────┬─────┘                                     │\n105\t│         │                                           │\n106\t│         ▼                                           │\n107\t│   ┌───────────┐                                     │\n108\t│   │ ⏳ EXECUTE │                                     │\n109\t│   └─────┬─────┘                                     │\n110\t│         │                                           │\n111\t│         ▼                                           │\n112\t│   ┌───────────┐                                     │\n113\t│   │ 📊 OUTCOME │                                     │\n114\t│   └─────┬─────┘                                     │\n115\t│         │                                           │\n116\t│         ├── ✅ success ──► close bead ──────────────┘\n117\t│         ├── ❌ failure ──► log + release ────────────┘\n118\t│         ├── ⏰ timeout ──► release + defer ──────────┘\n119\t│         └── 💀 crash ────► release + alert ──────────┘\n120\t│                                                     │\n121\t└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n122\t```\n123\t\n124\t---\n125\t\n126\t## 📐 Algorithm Steps\n127\t\n128\t### 🔍 Step 1: Select\n129\t\n130\tQuery the bead queue for the next claimable bead in **deterministic priority order**. Selection is not random — given the same queue state, every worker computes the same ordering. Ties are broken by creation time (oldest first).\n131\t\n132\t### 🔒 Step 2: Claim\n133\t\n134\tAttempt an **atomic claim** via `br update --claim`. SQLite transaction isolation guarantees exactly one worker succeeds. If the claim fails (race lost), return to Step 1 with the losing candidate excluded.\n135\t\n136\t### 📋 Step 3: Build\n137\t\n138\tConstruct the prompt from the bead's context: title, body, workspace path, relevant files, and any dependency context. The prompt is a deterministic function of the bead state — same bead, same prompt.\n139\t\n140\t### 🚀 Step 4: Dispatch\n141\t\n142\tLoad the agent adapter configuration (YAML), render the invoke template with the built prompt, and execute via `bash -c`. The agent runs headless — it receives a prompt, does work, and exits.\n143\t\n144\t### ⏳ Step 5: Execute\n145\t\n146\tThe agent runs. NEEDLE waits. The only inputs are the exit code and stdout/stderr. There is no interactive communication during execution.\n147\t\n148\t### 📊 Step 6: Outcome\n149\t\n150\tEvaluate the result and follow the **explicit path** for the observed outcome:\n151\t\n152\t| Outcome | Exit Code | Handler |\n153\t|---------|-----------|---------|\n154\t| ✅ **Success** | `0` | Validate output → close bead → log effort → **loop** |\n155\t| ❌ **Failure** | `1` | Log failure reason → release bead → increment retry count → **loop** |\n156\t| ⏰ **Timeout** | `124` | Release bead → mark deferred → **loop** |\n157\t| 💀 **Crash** | `>128` | Release bead → create alert bead → **loop** |\n158\t| 🏁 **Race Lost** | `4` | (Handled at Step 2) → exclude candidate → **retry select** |\n159\t| 🫙 **Queue Empty** | — | Enter strand escalation → **explore / mend / knot** |\n160\t\n161\tEvery row is implemented. There are no unhandled cases.\n162\t\n163\t---\n164\t\n165\t## 🧶 Strand Escalation\n166\t\n167\tWhen the primary workspace has no claimable beads, NEEDLE follows a **strand sequence** to find or create work. Each strand is evaluated in order — the first strand that yields a bead wins.\n168\t\n169\t| # | Strand | Agent? | Purpose |\n170\t|---|--------|--------|---------|\n171\t| 1 | 🪡 **Pluck** | Yes | Process beads from the assigned workspace |\n172\t| 2 | 🔧 **Mend** | No | Cleanup: orphaned claims, stale locks, health checks |\n173\t| 3 | 🔭 **Explore** | No | Search other workspaces for claimable beads |\n174\t| 4 | 🕸️ **Weave** | Yes | Create beads from documentation gaps *(opt-in)* |\n175\t| 5 | 🪢 **Unravel** | Yes | Propose alternatives for HUMAN-blocked beads *(opt-in)* |\n176\t| 6 | 💓 **Pulse** | Yes | Codebase health scans, auto-generate beads *(opt-in)* |\n177\t| 7 | 🪞 **Reflect** | Yes | Consolidate learnings from recent beads *(opt-in)* |\n178\t| 8 | 🪡 **Splice** | No | Document worker failures, create alert beads |\n179\t| 9 | 🪢 **Knot** | No | All strands exhausted — alert human, wait |\n180\t\n181\t---\n182\t\n183\t## ⚡ Parallel Workers\n184\t\n185\tMultiple NEEDLE workers run independently with **no central orchestrator**. Coordination happens through the shared bead queue:\n186\t\n187\t- **Atomicity** — `br update --claim` uses SQLite transactions; exactly one worker wins each claim\n188\t- **Determinism** — all workers compute the same priority order; races are resolved by the database, not by timing\n189\t- **Independence** — each worker is a self-contained loop in its own tmux session\n190\t- **Naming** — workers use NATO alphabet identifiers: `alpha`, `bravo`, `charlie`, ...\n191\t\n192\t```\n193\t  needle-claude-sonnet-alpha ──┐\n194\t  needle-claude-sonnet-bravo ──┤\n195\t  needle-codex-gpt4-charlie ───┼──► Shared .beads/ (SQLite + JSONL)\n196\t  needle-opencode-qwen-delta ──┤\n197\t  needle-aider-sonnet-echo ────┘\n198\t```\n199\t\n200\t---\n201\t\n202\t## 🏗️ Supported Agents\n203\t\n204\tNEEDLE is agent-agnostic. Any CLI that accepts a prompt and exits works.\n205\t\n206\t| Agent | CLI | Input Method | Notes |\n207\t|-------|-----|-------------|-------|\n208\t| Claude Code (interactive) | `claude-interactive` | stdin | **Recommended** — uses subscription billing; see [plugin](#-claude-interactive-plugin) |\n209\t| Claude Code (API) | `claude --print` | stdin | Uses programmatic/API billing |\n210\t| OpenCode | `opencode` | file | |\n211\t| Codex CLI | `codex` | args | |\n212\t| Aider | `aider --message` | args | |\n213\t| *Custom* | *any* | *configurable via YAML adapter* | |\n214\t\n215\tAdding a new agent requires **only a YAML configuration file** — no code changes.\n216\t\n217\t---\n218\t\n219\t## 🔌 claude-interactive Plugin\n220\t\n221\tThe `claude-interactive` plugin ships as a separate release asset. It wraps the Claude Code CLI in a PTY so workers run under your **Claude subscription** instead of consuming programmatic API credits.\n222\t\n223\t**How it works:** NEEDLE pipes subprocess stdio, which causes `claude` to detect a non-TTY and switch to API billing. `claude-interactive` creates an internal PTY so `claude` sees a real terminal, keeping it in interactive/subscription mode.\n224\t\n225\t**Install:**\n226\t\n227\t```bash\n228\t# Download the latest claude-interactive release\n229\tgh release download --repo jedarden/NEEDLE --pattern 'claude-interactive*'\n230\tchmod +x claude-interactive-install.sh\n231\t./claude-interactive-install.sh\n232\t```\n233\t\n234\t**Requirements:** Python 3.10+, `pyte` (`pip install pyte`), and the `claude` CLI on PATH.\n235\t\n236\t**Run:**\n237\t\n238\t```bash\n239\tcd /path/to/workspace\n240\tneedle run --agent claude-interactive --count 4\n241\t```\n242\t\n243\tSource lives in [`plugins/claude-interactive/`](plugins/claude-interactive/).\n244\t\n245\t---\n246\t\n247\t## 📁 Repository Structure\n248\t\n249\t```\n250\tNEEDLE/\n251\t├── Cargo.toml             # Rust crate manifest\n252\t├── install.sh             # One-line installer for prebuilt binaries\n253\t├── plugins/\n254\t│   └── claude-interactive/ # PTY wrapper — subscription billing adapter for Claude Code\n255\t├── src/\n256\t│   ├── main.rs            # Worker entry point\n257\t│   ├── lib.rs             # Library root\n258\t│   ├── agent_event.rs     # Agent event telemetry utilities\n259\t│   ├── claude_md_placement.rs  # CLAUDE.md placement logic\n260\t│   ├── commit_hook.rs     # Bead-Id trailer injection for git commits\n261\t│   ├── routing.rs         # Model-based adapter routing\n262\t│   ├── bead_store/        # Abstract bead backend interface\n263\t│   ├── bin/               # Auxiliary binaries (transform helpers)\n264\t│   ├── canary/            # Release channel promotion, canary tests\n265\t│   ├── claim/             # Atomic bead claiming via SQLite transactions\n266\t│   ├── cli/               # Command-line interface parsing\n267\t│   ├── config/            # `.needle.yaml` parsing and defaults\n268\t│   ├── cost/              # Token + USD spend tracking per bead and worker\n269\t│   ├── decision/          # Decision point detection, ADR management\n270\t│   ├── dispatch/          # Agent invocation + YAML adapter loading\n271\t│   ├── drift/             # Session similarity, clustering, divergence detection\n272\t│   ├── health/            # Liveness, stale-claim cleanup, watchdog\n273\t│   ├── learning/          # Retrospective extraction, learnings management\n274\t│   ├── mitosis/           # Child-aware bead splitting\n275\t│   ├── outcome/           # Explicit handler per outcome type\n276\t│   ├── peer/              # Multi-worker coordination, peer discovery\n277\t│   ├── prompt/            # Deterministic prompt construction from bead\n278\t│   ├── rate_limit/        # Provider/model concurrency and RPM rate limiting\n279\t│   ├── registry/          # Worker state registry\n280\t│   ├── sanitize/          # Output redaction (gitleaks integration)\n281\t│   ├── skill/             # Skill library, retrieval, promotion\n282\t│   ├── span/              # W3C trace context utilities\n283\t│   ├── stats/             # Aggregation engine, A/B comparison\n284\t│   ├── strand/            # Pluck / Mend / Explore / Weave / Unravel / Pulse / Reflect / Splice / Knot\n285\t│   ├── supervisor/        # Fleet supervisor daemon (auto-scale)\n286\t│   ├── telemetry/         # OTLP exporter, gen_ai semantic conventions\n287\t│   ├── trace/             # Trace capture, storage, retention\n288\t│   ├── transcript/        # Session JSONL parsing, action-outcome extraction\n289\t│   ├── types/             # Shared types, error definitions\n290\t│   ├── upgrade/           # Self-update, hot-reload, rollback\n291\t│   ├── validation/        # Pre-dispatch and post-execution checks\n292\t│   └── worker/            # Worker session and identity management\n293\t├── tests/                 # Integration tests\n294\t├── ci/                    # Docker images used by GitHub Actions\n295\t├── .github/workflows/     # CI + release pipelines\n296\t├── config/                # Vendored gitleaks rules\n297\t└── docs/                  # Plan, research, examples, post-mortems\n298\t```\n299\t\n300\t---\n301\t\n302\t## 🔍 Observability\n303\t\n304\tNEEDLE emits structured telemetry for every state transition, claim attempt, dispatch, and outcome. A silent worker is a broken worker.\n305\t\n306\t### Exported Signals\n307\t\n308\t| Signal | Description |\n309\t|--------|-------------|\n310\t| **Traces** | Spans for `worker.session`, `bead.lifecycle`, `bead.claim`, `agent.dispatch`, `strand.evaluated`, `outcome.handled` |\n311\t| **Metrics** | `needle.beads.completed`, `needle.beads.duration`, `needle.agent.tokens.input`, `needle.cost.usd`, and more |\n312\t| **Logs** | All events not represented as spans, with severity mapping (`ERROR` for failures, `WARN` for stale peers) |\n313\t\n314\t### OpenTelemetry (OTLP) Export\n315\t\n316\tNEEDLE can export telemetry to any OpenTelemetry-compatible backend (Jaeger, Tempo, Grafana, Honeycomb, Datadog, etc.) via OTLP.\n317\t\n318\t**Minimal configuration (`.needle.yaml`):**\n319\t\n320\t```yaml\n321\ttelemetry:\n322\t  otlp_sink:\n323\t    enabled: true\n324\t    endpoint: \"http://localhost:4317\"  # gRPC, or :4318 for HTTP\n325\t    protocol: \"grpc\"\n326\t```\n327\t\n328\t**Semantic conventions:** NEEDLE follows OpenTelemetry's `gen_ai.*` semantic conventions for LLM telemetry, enabling out-of-the-box integration with GenAI dashboards (Grafana GenAI app, Langfuse, Honeycomb AI, etc.).\n329\t\n330\tSee [`docs/plan/plan.md`](docs/plan/plan.md) for the complete semantic mapping table.\n331\t\n332\t---\n333\t\n334\t## 📊 Production Status\n335\t\n336\tNEEDLE currently powers my own headless multi-agent workflow — workers run continuously against shared bead queues, dispatching to Claude Code and other CLIs, with full OTLP telemetry wired through. APIs are stable enough that I rebuild on top of them daily.\n337\t\n338\tThis is alpha software in the sense that **I'm the primary user**, not in the sense that \"it doesn't work.\" Resource governance is delegated to [claude-governor](https://github.com/jedarden/claude-governor); session monitoring is handled by [ccdash](https://github.com/jedarden/ccdash).\n339\t\n340\tIf you want to run NEEDLE in your own workflow, open an issue and I'll help.\n341\t\n342\t---\n343\t\n344\t## 🔗 Related Projects\n345\t\n346\t- **[claude-governor](https://github.com/jedarden/claude-governor)** — caps API spend and enforces weekly Anthropic quotas across NEEDLE worker fleets\n347\t- **[ccdash](https://github.com/jedarden/ccdash)** — TUI for monitoring Claude Code sessions, token usage, and worker activity\n348\t- **[CLASP](https://github.com/jedarden/CLASP)** — drop-in proxy letting Claude Code target OpenAI, Gemini, Anthropic, or any LLM backend\n349\t- **[agentists-quickstart](https://github.com/jedarden/agentists-quickstart)** — opinionated DevPod workspaces for running Claude Code + NEEDLE\n350\t\n351\t---\n352\t\n353\t## 📄 License\n354\t\n355\tMIT\n356\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"f529f17d-ba9c-4a14-bc7d-2aec286f9f85","uuid":"70d9e33d-bb14-448e-bb86-df4eb9409915","timestamp":"2026-07-09T04:43:21.341Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/NEEDLE/README.md","content":"![NEEDLE](docs/hero.png)\n\n# 🧵 NEEDLE\n\n[![CI](https://github.com/jedarden/NEEDLE/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/jedarden/NEEDLE/actions/workflows/ci.yml)\n[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg)](LICENSE)\n[![Rust](https://img.shields.io/badge/rust-1.75+-orange.svg)](rust-toolchain.toml)\n[![Version](https://img.shields.io/badge/version-0.2.8-green.svg)](Cargo.toml)\n\n**N**avigates **E**very **E**nqueued **D**eliverable, **L**ogs **E**ffort\n\n> Deterministic bead processing with explicit outcome paths.\n\nNEEDLE is a universal wrapper for headless coding CLI agents. It processes a shared bead queue in deterministic order, dispatching work to any headless CLI (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex, Aider) and handling every outcome through an explicit, predefined path.\n\n---\n\n## 🚀 Quickstart\n\n```bash\n# Install the latest release\ncurl -fsSL https://github.com/jedarden/NEEDLE/releases/latest/download/install.sh | bash\n\n# Or build from source\ncargo install --git https://github.com/jedarden/NEEDLE\n\n# Run a worker against a bead-tracked workspace\ncd /path/to/your/workspace\nneedle run --agent claude --identity alpha\n```\n\nA worker starts, claims the next bead, dispatches to your chosen agent CLI, and loops. Multiple workers can run in parallel against the same workspace — coordination is handled by the shared bead queue (no central orchestrator).\n\nSee [`docs/examples/`](docs/examples/) for end-to-end configurations.\n\n---\n\n## 🧶 What is a bead?\n\nA **bead** is a work item — the unit of work NEEDLE processes. Think of it as a structured task ticket: a title, a body describing the deliverable and acceptance criteria, a status (`open`, `in_progress`, `done`), and optional metadata like priority and dependencies.\n\nBeads live in a **bead store** — a SQLite database (`beads.db`) plus a JSONL checkpoint file (`issues.jsonl`) in a `.beads/` directory inside your project. The `br` CLI manages the bead store: create beads, list them, claim them, close them.\n\n```bash\n# Create a bead\nbr create --title \"Add pagination to search results\" --body \"...\"\n\n# List open beads\nbr list --status open\n\n# NEEDLE does the rest: claims, dispatches, and closes beads automatically\n```\n\nNEEDLE workers read from this store, claim the next available bead atomically, dispatch it to your chosen agent CLI, and close it on success — then loop.\n\n---\n\n## 🤔 Why NEEDLE\n\nExisting agent orchestration tools are built for one of two shapes:\n\n- **Conversational frameworks** (LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI) assume a chat loop with a human-in-the-loop or another LLM. They are bad at headless, long-running, cost-bounded work.\n- **Workflow engines** (Temporal, Argo Workflows, Inngest) assume each step is deterministic code. They are bad at non-deterministic agent steps whose outcomes have to be classified and routed.\n\nNEEDLE is the missing middle: a **deterministic state machine that drives non-deterministic agents.** Every outcome an agent can produce has an explicit handler. The agent's work is fuzzy; the orchestration around it is not.\n\n---\n\n## 🧠 Core Principle\n\nNEEDLE is a **state machine**, not a script. Every bead transitions through a finite set of states, and every transition has a defined handler. There are no implicit fallbacks, no swallowed errors, no undefined paths.\n\n```\nIf an outcome can happen, it has a handler.\nIf it doesn't have a handler, it cannot happen.\n```\n\n---\n\n## 🔄 The NEEDLE Algorithm\n\nA single worker executes this loop indefinitely:\n\n```\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│                                                     │\n│   ┌───────────┐                                     │\n│   │  🔍 SELECT │◄────────────────────────────────┐  │\n│   └─────┬─────┘                                  │  │\n│         │                                        │  │\n│         ▼                                        │  │\n│   ┌───────────┐   race lost    ┌──────────┐     │  │\n│   │  🔒 CLAIM  │──────────────►│ 🔁 RETRY  │─────┘  │\n│   └─────┬─────┘               └──────────┘        │\n│         │ claimed                                   │\n│         ▼                                           │\n│   ┌───────────┐                                     │\n│   │ 📋 BUILD   │                                     │\n│   └─────┬─────┘                                     │\n│         │                                           │\n│         ▼                                           │\n│   ┌───────────┐                                     │\n│   │ 🚀 DISPATCH│                                     │\n│   └─────┬─────┘                                     │\n│         │                                           │\n│         ▼                                           │\n│   ┌───────────┐                                     │\n│   │ ⏳ EXECUTE │                                     │\n│   └─────┬─────┘                                     │\n│         │                                           │\n│         ▼                                           │\n│   ┌───────────┐                                     │\n│   │ 📊 OUTCOME │                                     │\n│   └─────┬─────┘                                     │\n│         │                                           │\n│         ├── ✅ success ──► close bead ──────────────┘\n│         ├── ❌ failure ──► log + release ────────────┘\n│         ├── ⏰ timeout ──► release + defer ──────────┘\n│         └── 💀 crash ────► release + alert ──────────┘\n│                                                     │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n---\n\n## 📐 Algorithm Steps\n\n### 🔍 Step 1: Select\n\nQuery the bead queue for the next claimable bead in **deterministic priority order**. Selection is not random — given the same queue state, every worker computes the same ordering. Ties are broken by creation time (oldest first).\n\n### 🔒 Step 2: Claim\n\nAttempt an **atomic claim** via `br update --claim`. SQLite transaction isolation guarantees exactly one worker succeeds. If the claim fails (race lost), return to Step 1 with the losing candidate excluded.\n\n### 📋 Step 3: Build\n\nConstruct the prompt from the bead's context: title, body, workspace path, relevant files, and any dependency context. The prompt is a deterministic function of the bead state — same bead, same prompt.\n\n### 🚀 Step 4: Dispatch\n\nLoad the agent adapter configuration (YAML), render the invoke template with the built prompt, and execute via `bash -c`. The agent runs headless — it receives a prompt, does work, and exits.\n\n### ⏳ Step 5: Execute\n\nThe agent runs. NEEDLE waits. The only inputs are the exit code and stdout/stderr. There is no interactive communication during execution.\n\n### 📊 Step 6: Outcome\n\nEvaluate the result and follow the **explicit path** for the observed outcome:\n\n| Outcome | Exit Code | Handler |\n|---------|-----------|---------|\n| ✅ **Success** | `0` | Validate output → close bead → log effort → **loop** |\n| ❌ **Failure** | `1` | Log failure reason → release bead → increment retry count → **loop** |\n| ⏰ **Timeout** | `124` | Release bead → mark deferred → **loop** |\n| 💀 **Crash** | `>128` | Release bead → create alert bead → **loop** |\n| 🏁 **Race Lost** | `4` | (Handled at Step 2) → exclude candidate → **retry select** |\n| 🫙 **Queue Empty** | — | Enter strand escalation → **explore / mend / knot** |\n\nEvery row is implemented. There are no unhandled cases.\n\n---\n\n## 🧶 Strand Escalation\n\nWhen the primary workspace has no claimable beads, NEEDLE follows a **strand sequence** to find or create work. Each strand is evaluated in order — the first strand that yields a bead wins.\n\n| # | Strand | Agent? | Purpose |\n|---|--------|--------|---------|\n| 1 | 🪡 **Pluck** | Yes | Process beads from the assigned workspace |\n| 2 | 🔧 **Mend** | No | Cleanup: orphaned claims, stale locks, health checks |\n| 3 | 🔭 **Explore** | No | Search other workspaces for claimable beads |\n| 4 | 🕸️ **Weave** | Yes | Create beads from documentation gaps *(opt-in)* |\n| 5 | 🪢 **Unravel** | Yes | Propose alternatives for HUMAN-blocked beads *(opt-in)* |\n| 6 | 💓 **Pulse** | Yes | Codebase health scans, auto-generate beads *(opt-in)* |\n| 7 | 🪞 **Reflect** | Yes | Consolidate learnings from recent beads *(opt-in)* |\n| 8 | 🪡 **Splice** | No | Document worker failures, create alert beads |\n| 9 | 🪢 **Knot** | No | All strands exhausted — alert human, wait |\n\n---\n\n## ⚡ Parallel Workers\n\nMultiple NEEDLE workers run independently with **no central orchestrator**. Coordination happens through the shared bead queue:\n\n- **Atomicity** — `br update --claim` uses SQLite transactions; exactly one worker wins each claim\n- **Determinism** — all workers compute the same priority order; races are resolved by the database, not by timing\n- **Independence** — each worker is a self-contained loop in its own tmux session\n- **Naming** — workers use NATO alphabet identifiers: `alpha`, `bravo`, `charlie`, ...\n\n```\n  needle-claude-sonnet-alpha ──┐\n  needle-claude-sonnet-bravo ──┤\n  needle-codex-gpt4-charlie ───┼──► Shared .beads/ (SQLite + JSONL)\n  needle-opencode-qwen-delta ──┤\n  needle-aider-sonnet-echo ────┘\n```\n\n---\n\n## 🏗️ Supported Agents\n\nNEEDLE is agent-agnostic. Any CLI that accepts a prompt and exits works.\n\n| Agent | CLI | Input Method | Notes |\n|-------|-----|-------------|-------|\n| Claude Code (interactive) | `claude-interactive` | stdin | **Recommended** — uses subscription billing; see [plugin](#-claude-interactive-plugin) |\n| Claude Code (API) | `claude --print` | stdin | Uses programmatic/API billing |\n| OpenCode | `opencode` | file | |\n| Codex CLI | `codex` | args | |\n| Aider | `aider --message` | args | |\n| *Custom* | *any* | *configurable via YAML adapter* | |\n\nAdding a new agent requires **only a YAML configuration file** — no code changes.\n\n---\n\n## 🔌 claude-interactive Plugin\n\nThe `claude-interactive` plugin ships as a separate release asset. It wraps the Claude Code CLI in a PTY so workers run under your **Claude subscription** instead of consuming programmatic API credits.\n\n**How it works:** NEEDLE pipes subprocess stdio, which causes `claude` to detect a non-TTY and switch to API billing. `claude-interactive` creates an internal PTY so `claude` sees a real terminal, keeping it in interactive/subscription mode.\n\n**Install:**\n\n```bash\n# Download the latest claude-interactive release\ngh release download --repo jedarden/NEEDLE --pattern 'claude-interactive*'\nchmod +x claude-interactive-install.sh\n./claude-interactive-install.sh\n```\n\n**Requirements:** Python 3.10+, `pyte` (`pip install pyte`), and the `claude` CLI on PATH.\n\n**Run:**\n\n```bash\ncd /path/to/workspace\nneedle run --agent claude-interactive --count 4\n```\n\nSource lives in [`plugins/claude-interactive/`](plugins/claude-interactive/).\n\n---\n\n## 📁 Repository Structure\n\n```\nNEEDLE/\n├── Cargo.toml             # Rust crate manifest\n├── install.sh             # One-line installer for prebuilt binaries\n├── plugins/\n│   └── claude-interactive/ # PTY wrapper — subscription billing adapter for Claude Code\n├── src/\n│   ├── main.rs            # Worker entry point\n│   ├── lib.rs             # Library root\n│   ├── agent_event.rs     # Agent event telemetry utilities\n│   ├── claude_md_placement.rs  # CLAUDE.md placement logic\n│   ├── commit_hook.rs     # Bead-Id trailer injection for git commits\n│   ├── routing.rs         # Model-based adapter routing\n│   ├── bead_store/        # Abstract bead backend interface\n│   ├── bin/               # Auxiliary binaries (transform helpers)\n│   ├── canary/            # Release channel promotion, canary tests\n│   ├── claim/             # Atomic bead claiming via SQLite transactions\n│   ├── cli/               # Command-line interface parsing\n│   ├── config/            # `.needle.yaml` parsing and defaults\n│   ├── cost/              # Token + USD spend tracking per bead and worker\n│   ├── decision/          # Decision point detection, ADR management\n│   ├── dispatch/          # Agent invocation + YAML adapter loading\n│   ├── drift/             # Session similarity, clustering, divergence detection\n│   ├── health/            # Liveness, stale-claim cleanup, watchdog\n│   ├── learning/          # Retrospective extraction, learnings management\n│   ├── mitosis/           # Child-aware bead splitting\n│   ├── outcome/           # Explicit handler per outcome type\n│   ├── peer/              # Multi-worker coordination, peer discovery\n│   ├── prompt/            # Deterministic prompt construction from bead\n│   ├── rate_limit/        # Provider/model concurrency and RPM rate limiting\n│   ├── registry/          # Worker state registry\n│   ├── sanitize/          # Output redaction (gitleaks integration)\n│   ├── skill/             # Skill library, retrieval, promotion\n│   ├── span/              # W3C trace context utilities\n│   ├── stats/             # Aggregation engine, A/B comparison\n│   ├── strand/            # Pluck / Mend / Explore / Weave / Unravel / Pulse / Reflect / Splice / Knot\n│   ├── supervisor/        # Fleet supervisor daemon (auto-scale)\n│   ├── telemetry/         # OTLP exporter, gen_ai semantic conventions\n│   ├── trace/             # Trace capture, storage, retention\n│   ├── transcript/        # Session JSONL parsing, action-outcome extraction\n│   ├── types/             # Shared types, error definitions\n│   ├── upgrade/           # Self-update, hot-reload, rollback\n│   ├── validation/        # Pre-dispatch and post-execution checks\n│   └── worker/            # Worker session and identity management\n├── tests/                 # Integration tests\n├── ci/                    # Docker images used by GitHub Actions\n├── .github/workflows/     # CI + release pipelines\n├── config/                # Vendored gitleaks rules\n└── docs/                  # Plan, research, examples, post-mortems\n```\n\n---\n\n## 🔍 Observability\n\nNEEDLE emits structured telemetry for every state transition, claim attempt, dispatch, and outcome. A silent worker is a broken worker.\n\n### Exported Signals\n\n| Signal | Description |\n|--------|-------------|\n| **Traces** | Spans for `worker.session`, `bead.lifecycle`, `bead.claim`, `agent.dispatch`, `strand.evaluated`, `outcome.handled` |\n| **Metrics** | `needle.beads.completed`, `needle.beads.duration`, `needle.agent.tokens.input`, `needle.cost.usd`, and more |\n| **Logs** | All events not represented as spans, with severity mapping (`ERROR` for failures, `WARN` for stale peers) |\n\n### OpenTelemetry (OTLP) Export\n\nNEEDLE can export telemetry to any OpenTelemetry-compatible backend (Jaeger, Tempo, Grafana, Honeycomb, Datadog, etc.) via OTLP.\n\n**Minimal configuration (`.needle.yaml`):**\n\n```yaml\ntelemetry:\n  otlp_sink:\n    enabled: true\n    endpoint: \"http://localhost:4317\"  # gRPC, or :4318 for HTTP\n    protocol: \"grpc\"\n```\n\n**Semantic conventions:** NEEDLE follows OpenTelemetry's `gen_ai.*` semantic conventions for LLM telemetry, enabling out-of-the-box integration with GenAI dashboards (Grafana GenAI app, Langfuse, Honeycomb AI, etc.).\n\nSee [`docs/plan/plan.md`](docs/plan/plan.md) for the complete semantic mapping table.\n\n---\n\n## 📊 Production Status\n\nNEEDLE currently powers my own headless multi-agent workflow — workers run continuously against shared bead queues, dispatching to Claude Code and other CLIs, with full OTLP telemetry wired through. APIs are stable enough that I rebuild on top of them daily.\n\nThis is alpha software in the sense that **I'm the primary user**, not in the sense that \"it doesn't work.\" Resource governance is delegated to [claude-governor](https://github.com/jedarden/claude-governor); session monitoring is handled by [ccdash](https://github.com/jedarden/ccdash).\n\nIf you want to run NEEDLE in your own workflow, open an issue and I'll help.\n\n---\n\n## 🔗 Related Projects\n\n- **[claude-governor](https://github.com/jedarden/claude-governor)** — caps API spend and enforces weekly Anthropic quotas across NEEDLE worker fleets\n- **[ccdash](https://github.com/jedarden/ccdash)** — TUI for monitoring Claude Code sessions, token usage, and worker activity\n- **[CLASP](https://github.com/jedarden/CLASP)** — drop-in proxy letting Claude Code target OpenAI, Gemini, Anthropic, or any LLM backend\n- **[agentists-quickstart](https://github.com/jedarden/agentists-quickstart)** — opinionated DevPod workspaces for running Claude Code + NEEDLE\n\n---\n\n## 📄 License\n\nMIT\n","numLines":356,"startLine":1,"totalLines":356}}}

This document describes all available debug flags, logging configuration options, and how to enable filtering-related debug output.\n\n## Logging Infrastructure\n\nPluck uses Rust's `tracing` crate for structured logging with the `RUST_LOG` environment variable controlling output verbosity and scope.\n\n### Environment Variable\n\n**`RUST_LOG`** - Controls logging levels for specific Rust modules\n\n**Syntax:** `RUST_LOG=module_path=log_level[,module_path=log_level,...]`\n\n**Available Log Levels:**\n- `error` - Only errors\n- `warn` - Warnings and errors  \n- `info` - Normal informational messages (default)\n- `debug` - Detailed diagnostic information\n- `trace` - Extremely detailed execution flow\n\n## Available Debug Modules\n\n### Pluck-Specific Modules\n\n| Module Path | Description | Recommended Level |\n|-------------|-------------|-------------------|\n| `needle::strand::pluck` | Core Pluck strand evaluation logic | `debug` or `trace` |\n| `needle::strand` | All strand coordination (Pluck, Mend, Explore, etc.) | `debug` |\n| `needle::worker` | Worker state machine and lifecycle | `debug` |\n\n### Supporting Modules\n\n| Module Path | Description | Recommended Level |\n|-------------|-------------|-------------------|\n| `needle::bead_store` | Bead store queries and operations | `debug` |\n| `needle::dispatch` | Agent dispatch and execution | `debug` |\n| `needle::claim` | Bead claiming logic | `debug` |\n\n## Debug Configuration Presets\n\n### 1. Minimal Pluck Debug\n**Purpose:** Filter decisions and candidate counts only\n\n```bash\nexport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug\n```\n\n**What you'll see:**\n- Pluck strand evaluation starting\n- Bead store query results\n- Candidate counts after filtering\n- Label filtering exclusions\n- Final candidate return\n\n### 2. Comprehensive Pluck Trace  \n**Purpose:** Detailed Pluck execution flow with all filtering decisions\n\n```bash\nexport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug\n```\n\n**What you'll see:**\n- Everything from minimal debug, plus:\n- Individual excluded bead details with labels and reasons\n- Status/assignee filtering details\n- Split threshold evaluation\n- Detailed candidate sorting information\n\n### 3. Full Worker Context (RECOMMENDED)\n**Purpose:** Complete debugging context including coordination and storage\n\n```bash\nexport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug,needle::dispatch=debug\n```\n\n**What you'll see:**\n- All Pluck trace output\n- Worker state transitions\n- Bead store operations\n- Agent dispatch details\n- Claim retry logic\n\n### 4. Maximum Debug Output\n**Purpose:** Everything at debug level (not recommended - very verbose)\n\n```bash\nexport RUST_LOG=debug\n```\n\n## Key Pluck Debug Events\n\n### Strand Evaluation Start\n```\n[timestamp] DEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Pluck strand evaluation starting\n  exclude_labels: [\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"]\n  split_threshold: 3\n```\n\n### Bead Store Query\n```\n[timestamp] DEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Querying bead store for ready candidates\n  filters: Filters { assignee: None, exclude_labels: [\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"] }\n```\n\n### Label Filtering\n```\n[timestamp] DEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Label filtering excluded 2 beads\n  excluded_count: 2\n  remaining: 5\n  excluded_labels: [\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"]\n```\n\n### Individual Excluded Beads\n```\n[timestamp] DEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Excluded bead due to labels\n  bead_id: \"bf-1234\"\n  labels: [\"deferred\", \"bug-fix\"]\n  excluded_reasons: [\"deferred\"]\n```\n\n### Status/Assignee Filtering\n```\n[timestamp] DEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Status/assignee filtering removed 1 beads\n  filtered_count: 1\n  remaining: 4\n```\n\n### Split Trigger Evaluation\n```\n[timestamp] DEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Checking split trigger for first candidate\n  bead_id: \"bf-5678\"\n  failure_count: 2\n  threshold: 3\n  split_triggered: false\n```\n\n### Candidate Return\n```\n[timestamp] INFO needle::strand::pluck: Returning 3 candidates for processing\n  count: 3\n  candidates: [\"bf-1001\", \"bf-1002\", \"bf-1003\"]\n```\n\n## Filtering Decision Logging\n\nThe Pluck strand logs comprehensive filtering decisions at multiple points:\n\n### 1. Store Query Results (Line 117-120 in pluck.rs)\n```rust\ntracing::debug!(\n    filters = ?filters,\n    \"Querying bead store for ready candidates\"\n);\n```\n\n### 2. Initial Candidate Count (Line 124-128)\n```rust\ntracing::debug!(\n    count = beads.len(),\n    \"Bead store returned {} candidates\",\n    beads.len()\n);\n```\n\n### 3. Label Filtering Summary (Line 153-159)\n```rust\ntracing::debug!(\n    excluded_count = before_label_filter - after_label_filter,\n    remaining = after_label_filter,\n    excluded_labels = ?self.exclude_labels,\n    \"Label filtering excluded {} beads\",\n    before_label_filter - after_label_filter\n);\n```\n\n### 4. Individual Excluded Beads (Line 161-177)\n```rust\nfor (id, labels) in excluded_beads {\n    let excluded_reasons: Vec<_> = labels.iter()\n        .filter(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l))\n        .map(|l| l.as_str())\n        .collect();\n    tracing::debug!(\n        bead_id = %id,\n        labels = ?labels,\n        excluded_reasons = ?excluded_reasons,\n        \"Excluded bead due to labels\"\n    );\n}\n```\n\n### 5. Status/Assignee Filtering (Line 198-210)\n```rust\ntracing::debug!(\n    filtered_count = before_status_filter - after_status_filter,\n    remaining = after_status_filter,\n    \"Status/assignee filtering removed {} beads\",\n    before_status_filter - after_status_filter\n);\n```\n\n## Usage Examples\n\n### Direct Execution\n```bash\n# Set debug configuration\nexport RUST_LOG=\"needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug\"\n\n# Run NEEDLE worker\nneedle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1\n```\n\n### Using the Capture Script\n```bash\n# The ARMOR workspace includes a pre-configured capture script\n./capture-pluck-debug.sh /home/coding/ARMOR pluck-debug.log 1\n```\n\n### Sourcing Environment Configuration\n```bash\n# Use the pre-configured environment file\nsource .env.pluck-debug\n\n# Run NEEDLE worker\nneedle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1\n```\n\n### Temporary Override\n```bash\n# One-time debug execution without persistence\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1\n```\n\n## Log File Analysis\n\n### Filtering Pluck Output\n```bash\n# See only Pluck strand messages\ngrep -i 'pluck' pluck-debug.log\n\n# See only filtering decisions  \ngrep -i 'filter' pluck-debug.log\n\n# See only excluded beads\ngrep -i 'exclude' pluck-debug.log\n\n# See only candidate information\ngrep -i 'candidate' pluck-debug.log\n```\n\n### Analyzing Filtering Behavior\n```bash\n# Count excluded beads by label\ngrep \"excluded_labels\" pluck-debug.log | grep -oP 'excluded_count: \\K\\d+'\n\n# See which labels caused exclusions\ngrep \"excluded_reasons\" pluck-debug.log\n\n# Track candidate counts over time\ngrep \"Bead store returned\" pluck-debug.log\n```\n\n## Debug Output Interpretation\n\n### Normal Processing Flow\n1. **Pluck strand evaluation starting** - Worker began processing\n2. **Querying bead store for ready candidates** - Asking for available work\n3. **Bead store returned N candidates** - Found N potential beads\n4. **Label filtering excluded X beads** - Filtered out beads with excluded labels\n5. **Status/assignee filtering removed Y beads** - Filtered out unavailable beads\n6. **Returning Z candidates for processing** - Z beads remain after all filtering\n\n### No Work Available\n```\nPluck strand evaluation starting\nQuerying bead store for ready candidates\nBead store returned 0 candidates\nNo candidates remaining after filtering, returning NoWork\n```\n\n### Split Triggered\n```\nChecking split trigger for first candidate\n  bead_id: \"bf-retry-123\"\n  failure_count: 5\n  threshold: 3\n  split_triggered: true\n\nINFO needle::strand::pluck: Split threshold reached, returning Split instruction\n  bead_id: \"bf-retry-123\"\n  failure_count: 5\n  threshold: 3\n```\n\n## Configuration Files\n\n### `.env.pluck-debug` \nLocated at `/home/coding/ARMOR/.env.pluck-debug`\n\nThis file contains pre-configured debug settings:\n```bash\n# Minimal Pluck debug - filtering decisions and candidate counts\n# export RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug\n\n# Comprehensive Pluck trace - detailed execution flow\n# export RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace\n\n# Full strand context - all strands with detailed Pluck trace\n# export RUST_LOG=needle::strand=debug,needle::strand::pluck=trace\n\n# Complete worker context - Pluck + coordination + storage (RECOMMENDED)\nexport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug,needle::dispatch=debug\n```\n\n### `capture-pluck-debug.sh`\nLocated at `/home/coding/ARMOR/capture-pluck-debug.sh`\n\nAutomated script for capturing debug output:\n```bash\n./capture-pluck-debug.sh <workspace> <output_file> <count>\n```\n\n## Technical Details\n\n### Pluck Strand Source Location\nThe Pluck strand implementation is located at:\n```\n/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/strand/pluck.rs\n```\n\n### Default Exclude Labels\nWhen no custom exclude labels are provided, Pluck uses:\n```rust\nconst DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_LABELS: &[&str] = &[\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"];\n```\n\n### Default Split Threshold\n```rust\nsplit_after_failures: 3  // Triggers split after 3 consecutive failures\n```\n\n### Tracing Instrumentation Points\nThe Pluck strand uses `tracing::instrument!` macro (line 95-103) to automatically capture:\n- Strand name (\"pluck\")\n- Exclude labels configuration\n- Split threshold value\n- Function entry/exit timing\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### No Debug Output Appearing\n\n1. **Verify RUST_LOG is set:**\n   ```bash\n   echo $RUST_LOG\n   ```\n\n2. **Check for typos in module paths:**\n   ```bash\n   # Correct\n   RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug\n   \n   # Incorrect (missing namespace)\n   RUST_LOG=pluck=debug\n   ```\n\n3. **Ensure environment variable is exported:**\n   ```bash\n   # This won't work\n   RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug needle run\n   \n   # This will work\n   export RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug\n   needle run\n   ```\n\n### Too Much Output\n\n1. **Reduce scope to Pluck only:**\n   ```bash\n   export RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug\n   ```\n\n2. **Use specific log level:**\n   ```bash\n   # Only INFO and above\n   export RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=info\n   \n   # Only WARN and above  \n   export RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=warn\n   ```\n\n### Missing Specific Events\n\n1. **Check trace level for detailed events:**\n   ```bash\n   export RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace\n   ```\n\n2. **Enable supporting modules:**\n   ```bash\n   export RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::bead_store=debug\n   ```\n\n## Performance Considerations\n\n### Impact of Debug Logging\n- **`debug` level**: ~5-10% performance overhead\n- **`trace` level**: ~10-20% performance overhead  \n- **Full debug mode**: ~20-30% performance overhead\n\n### Disk Space Usage\nDebug logging can generate significant output:\n- **Minimal debug**: ~1-2 MB per hour per worker\n- **Full worker context**: ~5-10 MB per hour per worker\n- **Maximum debug**: ~20-50 MB per hour per worker\n\n## Related Documentation\n\n- **NEEDLE Project**: `/home/coding/NEEDLE/`\n- **Pluck Source**: `/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/strand/pluck.rs`\n- **Worker Module**: `/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/worker/mod.rs`\n- **Verify RUST_LOG is set:**\n   ```bash\n   echo $RUST_LOG\n   ```\n\n2. **Check for typos in module paths:**\n   ```bash\n   # Correct\n   RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug\n   \n   # Incorrect (missing namespace)\n   RUST_LOG=pluck=debug\n   ```\n\n3. **Ensure environment variable is exported:**\n   ```bash\n   # This won't work\n   RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug needle run\n   \n   # This will work\n   export RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug\n   needle run\n   ```\n\n### Too Much Output\n\n1. **Reduce scope to Pluck only:**\n   ```bash\n   export RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug\n   ```\n\n2. **Use specific log level:**\n   ```bash\n   # Only INFO and above\n   export RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=info\n   \n   # Only WARN and above  \n   export RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=warn\n   ```\n\n### Missing Specific Events\n\n1. **Check trace level for detailed events:**\n   ```bash\n   export RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace\n   ```\n\n2. **Enable supporting modules:**\n   ```bash\n   export RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::bead_store=debug\n   ```\n\n## Performance Considerations\n\n### Impact of Debug Logging\n- **`debug` level**: ~5-10% performance overhead\n- **`trace` level**: ~10-20% performance overhead  \n- **Full debug mode**: ~20-30% performance overhead\n\n### Disk Space Usage\nDebug logging can generate significant output:\n- **Minimal debug**: ~1-2 MB per hour per worker\n- **Full worker context**: ~5-10 MB per hour per worker\n- **Maximum debug**: ~20-50 MB per hour per worker\n\n## **Strand Coordination**: `/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/strand/mod.rs`\n- **ARMOR Configuration**: `/home/coding/ARMOR/.env.pluck-debug`\n- **Capture Script**:
